Great bathing suit! It has a beautiful style for the lucky curvy ones among us and can also help slim one down (especially in the abdomen area) for the chunkier ones among us. It has modest lines for more coverage than most bathing suits. That suits me fine as I'm no bathing beauty, LOL! The material is somewhat thick, which makes it durable and helps hold in imperfections. It has interior bra padding which I believe can be removed if desired. I want comfort in a bathing suit, and it delivers. I found the suit comfortable without any pulling around the legs, crotch, or shoulder areas. This bathing suit features a small silver clasp at the upper back to join two small straps. It's a bit challenging to connect and open the clasp because of its design and also how it's positioned. SIZE SUGGESTION: I ordered an XL based on the size chart. I am normally a Medium or sometimes a Large. I wasn't sure what fit I'd get by following the size chart; it turned out to be a PERFECT choice. I'm 5'4-1/2" with a little chunkiness around hips and abdomen at the time of purchase; the XL torso fit great as well as the bust area. So, the size chart measurements were true to size for me. That means I ordered 1-2 sizes larger than my usual. This suit is not made for someone with plus-size measurements as I ordered the largest size available. It would be a nice suit for plus sizes so perhaps one day it will be offered in additional sizes. WASHING: The tag recommends handwashing this nylon/Spandex suit and line drying it. This is a quality bathing suit and will delight its wearer for a long time with the right care. I hope my information is .

Great, great, great tummy control bathing suit!I do have mixed emotions about this bathing suit. I’ll tell you why in a second. But first, for your perspective, I am a 40 y/o mother of three. My height is 5’7” and I weigh 186 lbs. My bra size is 40B. I am normally a size 12 or large. In the sizing guide for this bathing suit a size 12 is an XL, so I ordered an XL. The bathing suit fits like a glove.This bathing suit is really, really good quality. The material is strong, thick enough to control my main problem area, my tummy. Really amazing tummy control. Loved that from the get-go. The rushed tummy area is a miracle maker.The boobage area is fantastic. The cups help those like us who weren’t too blessed in that department and the cleavage is sexy, yet conservative. The straps are fantastic. They are adjustable and feel really comfortable. The cut of the bathing suit is not too low, but not too high. And the strings are adorable. It’s a bit cheeky in the butt area, but again, conservative enough. I think you can modify that as you wish with the strings. If you want them higher or lower. I’ll have to play with that.The back has a clasp as the closure, and I love that. It really is so comfortable. I can’t stand halter tops because they hurt my neck, so this bathing suit is everything I love.Here is where my problem lies: the cutout design in the back. As gorgeous as it is on super models and super athletic and thin women, I feel that for the average women with their beautiful mom bods, this cutout sits right where the rolls are the strongest. I am sooooo bummed out about that. I absolutely love this bathing suit, and my back rolls just are too exposed.I may or may not wear this bathing suit. I guess I have to gain the confidence to wear it and not care about the back rolls. Other than that, this bathing suit is a home run. Absolutely perfect.

Shipping - The dress arrived in a timely manner - within two days with Prime. Not stuffed in the mailbox this time - you’re finally getting it Amazon.Packaging - The dress was well packaged in Amazon’s bubble padded soft envelope. It came in it’s own plastic, as well.Quality - The product itself is typical swimwear quality, perhaps slightly less.Fit - I’m 5”0” and 155 lbs - ugh! I know, it’s too much. I’m usually a 12/14 in clothes if I still want to breathe while wearing them. Size medium in most clothes don't fit well any more. It seems too tight and makes me feel self-conscious. I usually spend the entire time pulling fabrics away from my body when I wear something that’s a medium. Large seems to be the best fit overall. So, in this case, I ordered a large. However, the swimdress did not fit that well. It was loose all over. I like to think that I lost some weight from when the dress was ordered and when it arrived, but that’s not likely. So, if I had to re-order, I would choose a medium(?). I’m just afraid it might have been too tight and I’d be back trying to pull fabrics away from the body. But, then again, maybe not because the flare on the dress could take care of that for me. But, would I have felt like I could breathe? I didn’t want to spend money on something that would make me self-conscious and uncomfortable, so I opted for the large. No regrets here.Style - The style is amazing. It’s just what I wanted. Something that doesn’t stick to your body and show every little bit of fat that you work so hard to hide every other moment of your life. Something that flatters your figure. Something that covers up the hip area so you can maintain some dignity at the pool/beach. Love it! Would definitely buy again.

Reference: 5’9 170lbs and size XL Likes: 1. Longer length shorts are modest; they are also very comfortable. No worries about your cheeks hanging out or awkward grabbing at upper thighs. 2. Built in bra is comfortable and holds the breasts in place. 3. Adjustable length top (drawstrings); makes it customizable if you have a longer or shorter torso. 4. Overall comfort—the suit it very comfortable to wear and I do feel confident in it. Dislikes: 1. Built-in PANTY creates lines where it digs into upper thighs and potentially across the cheeks. I think that these could reasonably be cut out of the suit though… 2. Waist line on shorts—it’s a strange construction with a weird hen. It sticks out through the shirt and shows if you belly sticks out in the slightest. I wish it was more tummy-flattering and smoothing, especially with my c-section pooch. 3. Print/pattern is a little cheesy. I picked the navy blue suit with diagonal stripe pattern on it. When it arrived, I saw that it was a silly, galaxy pattern with shooting stars—it’s just a bit cheap looking, which the picture on Amazon doesn’t really reflect. Overall impression: Yes, I would buy this suit again—for the price point and the functionality, I am happy with it.

Can a swimsuit be a Godsend? If so, this is it for me. I was shopping for something with underwire that would also keep “the girls” in place while I roughhouse with my little boys in the pool, but that would also cover my belly up to at least my belly button. This suit doesn’t have underwire (see the pics) but the combination of the crisscross pieces over the bust coupled with the wireless cups, really gives the girls a nice shape and holds them in place. I can’t emphasize the magic of the design enough. Normally my girls look like a mashed-up sports bra squished mess in a bathing suit, but not in this one. I really love the little peep in the front and the way the back straps cross and tie. The adjustable straps are amazing as well. The front of the suit gives pretty decent tummy control. The back covers a little more booty than I’d like, but I can move the sides in a little to show a little more cheek without it looking weird or too deliberate. Swimsuit shopping is normally a depressing nightmare for me, so I had to post this review immediately for anyone else looking for a bathing suit that seems to do it all. For reference, I am 5’10” and about 190lbs. I ordered a Blue Striped in XL, which is what Amazon recommended for me based on previous clothing purchases and it fits perfectly.

I have ordered a number of swimsuits from Amazon with varying satisfaction, which I chalk up to my odd sized body. I am mid-60's, short, plump through the mid-section and small on top. I've lost more than 100 lbs over the past few years, and have sagging skin as a result. As expected the breast pads are huge in this suit, but fortunately they are removable. I crossed the back straps to make the top fit better and, like another reviewer, could NOT figure out the tie for the skirt. I was about to cut the tie out entirely when I read her review so I gave it another try and I think I have it figured out now.Now lets talk sizing: I really wish every reviewer would give their height and weight when reviewing sizes. I am 5'4" and about 165lbs. I wear a US Large/size 12 in nearly all clothing, but after reading the sizing advice on this item, I sized up to an XL. I wish I'd ordered a Large, though. The seat is a little loose thus the suit rides up a bit, and I worry once it is wet, it will be even worse. The cups, as I mentioned above, are very large. Without the inserted pads, there's almost too much fabric over my chest now and my uneven breasts are noticeable. I'll try to find some better pads that fit my cup sizes better. Aside from all the accommodations, this suit is attractive, particularly for mature women. The built-in skirt hides my upper thighs, which I find unattractive after so much weight loss. The coffee color is pretty; an almost purple brown. I may order a size large in another color and see if it fits better though.UPDATE: I ordered this suit, in blue and a size Large and it fits much better. The pads are still way too big for me but I can work with that. I wish I hadn't sized up at first, but glad I got a new one in the right size that I like.

I’m 5’9”, 180lbs, 36G bra size. This was the first suit I’d tried on in this price range that actually felt like quality material that might last a while. The top is very form-fitted and “clingy,” not the type of tankini that will float around you in the water. It has thin adjustable straps (they cannot be disconnected/crossed). Despite the thinness of the straps, they are nicely seamed and quite comfortable. There is a pretty supportive shelf bralette with round, removable pads. I have a large, non-perky chest, and I was pleasantly surprised by how well my chest was covered and supported. I wouldn’t go for a jog, but a family game of water polo won’t risk freeing the girls. Top is long, can be ruched up to flatter a middle-aged waist without any danger of showing off middle-aged belly flesh. The bottom is high-waisted without being ridiculous, and offers solid medium coverage, has a bit of cheekiness without letting a cheek slip out easily. There is very little “bite” to the waistband (and I’m prone to muffin top), with comfortable seams. The color block pattern is fun and bold. I will own at least one or two more of these suits. Excellent value for the price!

Being pregnant, its been hard to find flattering clothing let alone swim wear and I had to leave a positive review for my new favorite swimsuit! Size fit: I am currently 7 months, 5’5, 185 pounds pregnant. My cup size has ballooned to 38 DDD. Chest: I feel well supported with the pads. They don’t seem to be removable and there is an underwire. Based on the reviews, I went a size up to reduce the risk of the boning poking me since I busty. For less busty women, this item fits true to size and I wouldn’t recommend any looser since the shoulder straps are not adjustable. Silhouette: Slides over my belly and creates a cute baby doll look. I appreciate being able pull the shorts lower and hiding problem areas. Design: Color is bold and hasn’t faded. Patterns are flattering if you are trying to hide a little belly. All in all, you are getting more for a typical swimsuit in this price range. You won’t be disappointed.

First of all, the color was WAY darker than I was expecting. It looked more deep shiny teal in the pictures. It's still a lovely color, I just might have gone with the burgundy if I'd known the true color. The app suggested XL would fit me best, and despite my leanings towards my usual size L, I went with the XL suggestion based on some reviews. I'd say if you're more well endowed up top, go for the larger size. For me, I feel like Large would have fit me well. I'm 5'6", 190 lbs, somewhat athletic build, still recovering from having twins, pear shaped body type. I'm also wearing a new discovery in the picture to pair with the swim suit, they're swim leggings (YES, THAT'S A THING! 🙌🏼), Capri style, absolutely brilliant if your legs suffer from weight gain and cellulite like mine do. I'm ashamed of my legs and refuse to be photographed in shorts or a swim suit because of them, so for our upcoming vacation I found these brilliant leggings to accompany my new suit. I haven't worn them in the pool yet, just reviewing quickly for the other recent mom's like me looking to find a nice covering swim suit that doesn't accentuate the excess tummy skin for the upcoming dreaded swim suit season. If you have fit legs, the skirt part will be the perfect length for you. If you're average build and hold yourself with proper posture (shoulders back, chin up, tummy in, hips back) then it really helps you look your best and allows for the lovely pleating shown in the product image. (Pics 2 & 3) I will say though that the "shorts" section starts really low, leaving about 2" of "crackage" (I'm calling that now as the newest trend term) visible through the white middle liner section, so I'm glad I have the leggings to go over that for the inevitable skirt floatage in the pool water. I had a similar style maternity swim suit and the skirt always floated as high as the water level went, when fully imursed, it would float up to the seem level, so I expect this will do the same. I have twin toddlers now though, so they'll be clinging to me assisting in keeping the skirt section down. Just something to keep in mind as you shop for this style swim suit, as that can be embarrassing and downright annoying if you're not expecting it. This style is best for beach days or lounging pool side, not so much for public swimming pool regular swimming and water fun. The white section also is not a slimming type of material but lends well to ladies with more mid section or maternity bellies. I hope this review helped, if so, please click the button. Thanks! And happy shopping!

Ok, so this is going to be the long review about my body type that I wish I had. I am bottom heavy so dresses that fit my hips well are usually way to big on my chest. See my measurements below. Alright, alright, alright... This is a good one!!! I ordered this Aqua Eve Women High Waisted Bikini Set in an XL because I live in a swimsuit in the summer and like to change them like i change my daily clothes. This one is definitely one of my new favorites! For reference, I'm 5'5ish, 185ish, 38D and 30" waist... and I truly feel like this suit fit me like a glove! Here's what I like about it: •THE CUT! The cut of this suit is modest, fashion-forward and visually interesting. I feel like it is pretty flattering on my midsize body. Plus, it covers enough so that I can actually swim and play in the pool with the kids comfortably. The bow on the bottoms is fun, too. (It isn't super cheeky either.) •THE COLORS! These are colors that you just don't see on a suit very often. I love it! The rose pattern on the top is pretty and different. •WELL MADE! This suit is made of thick material that is structured, and stretchy, but you can't see any dimples underneath it. The cups in the top are removable-- which is what I always do, but to each their own. If you are expecting a suit with a ton of support up top, this isn't the one. But if you are wanting a suit that is super comfortable, easy to move around in, fun and different-- you should order it. I lightly recommend it!!!

I have been pregnant three times now, and I’ve gone through countless maternity bathing suits. Since I don’t want to spend tons of money on some thing that I will only wear for a shorter amount of time, I like to scan Amazon for the suits as opposed to designer brands. This one is the perfect one by far! The fabric isn’t super thick, but it’s ribbed and thick enough to feel like it’s holding you in, while also being very soft. I like how it doesn’t have a keyhole in the back, I like full upper back coverage. The straps are adjustable, which is a must have. The set overall is very nice and will leave you with extra room to grow. I am 5’4”, 30 weeks pregnant and about 200 pounds and got an XL. I could have easily gotten away with a large, but I wanted to leave room for the last 10 weeks where I know I will be growing more.

I’ve been looking for a bikini that was a tad skimpy but not a thong. We just moved back to Phoenix and have a pool and I plan on getting my tan on! Haha Price point was nice. Better than target prices or other websites that have a ton of high waisted bottoms. I’m so not into that style.. AT ALL! My husband hates the high waist trends that have been around too. Jeans, swim, everything. Yuck. I grew up in the low rise era haha! 04 ish time. I have 3 boys so I feel covered up but still sexy for my husband, vs frumpy, and able to show enough skin to work on my tan. My measurements: 38DD 33” waist 45” hip/size 12/ sometimes 10 dependent on brand. I’ve got a bubble butt and birthing hips lol! But also a curvy athletic build. The green is a nice soft sage/army green? Hard to describe but I love it and it was exactly what I was looking for! My eyes are green so I wanted something to help them pop as well as my tan while hanging out makeup-free by the pool all summer. :) I hand wash to keep color from fading. Very little soap… almost a spot wash. Hang dry as well. I’ve worn it about 10 times already and have been in and out of our clorinated pool a bunch and no fading. I’m careful not to sit on our cool decking or get up carefully so I don’t scratch up the material. Swimwear is prone to that… I saw other reviews but I think that’s just a given with swimwear. Be mindful. I also don’t rub up against my pool siding or sit on the seats or steps bc that it’s even more scratchy. Great price point to replace it when I wear this one down!! I love the scrunchy butt! Sexy but not too bad. I did have a tummy tuck after having 3 boys naturally! 9.9lbs, 10.9lbs and 10.2lbs all 22”. I grow big babies! :) hence my birthing hips lol! Back to what I was saying: this covers my TT scar! But I had an amazing Dr!! I’ve seen some gnarly Tt scars out there. I had some other bikinis last year and the year before that were thicker and others said were Tt safe, but I feel like they have too much coverage and aren’t as flattering. But they were still great! I just like this style of bottom better. Sexier. I just ordered the orange this AM too! Can’t wait! I highly recommend. Update: Got the orange one - same size and the top is a little smaller.. so it doesn’t look right on my 38DD.. pretty disappointed! So just an fyi.. however I’ll probably try another color… just beware of sizing… same brand/style and size but wasn’t correct in the right size… :( and without cups it’s seethru when wet- which doesn’t fly with my 3 little boys and their friends having pool time! 😬😬

Now I know I don't have too much bump yet, but I just turned 20 weeks and am starting to show a little. I've also gained weight, of course, and my legs are swollen from an 11hr day at work standing yesterday, so with that all in mind - here's my pics and review. I'm expecting a June baby, so I wanted a swimsuit at the ready. I loved the color options and had a hard time choosing, but I ended up going with the teal. The peekaboo belly is really cute, so are the scalloped edges and button details. The ruching makes me feel like I might even be able to use it postpartum if I want to swim and want to hide my belly until I lose what I put on. I am 5'9" and usually a 16/xl so I got the xl and find it true to size. The cut isn't too narrow at the crotch or on the cheeks so I appreciate the relatively modest coverage. I also really like that the fabric is thick and feels like a good quality swimsuit - not thin or see-through at all. It comes with removable cup liners and the shoulder straps are adjustable. For me at the moment, I felt like it could be more flattering, but maybe I'll like the cut more as it fits my growing bump. I would recommend it if you're looking for a quality suit with modest coverage.

Sharing the sage green color since there weren’t many examples of customer photos in green. It took longer to come in than the white one would have. The dress is definitely shapeless but I would wear it with just a few buttons clasped. This makes a great ‘fit when heading to the pool or short walk to the beach. You could probably even change out of your swim suit under here it’s so spacious. I do think it’s very over priced at almost $40. It’s not see-through, but could use steaming to get the wrinkles out. I am keeping this since I leave for my trip in less than a week. For reference I’m 5 ft 1 in tall, 211 lbs. 40D bust and it’s not tight in the back/arms if you’re only wearing your swim suit or a tank under it. Edit 3 weeks after purchase: Updated my review to 2 stars. This item is severely overpriced as it’s probably made by shein. I wore it daily to the pool and home in Hawaii and by day 4 one of the buttons near my lower half already came undone. $40 and you can’t even wear it for intended purpose or wash it without it breaking? After the first button came off I checked the others and they were all about to fall off as well. It’s nice and thick but wrinkles terribly and whats the point of a dress that doesn’t close- can’t even change out of your swimsuit underneath.

Note: the top is a separate item I am reviewing that just happens to match perfectly with this swim bottom.To start, the swim bottom fabric is comfortable, there's no itchy or scratchy feeling from it anywhere. However, the bottoms run small. How small? That would depend on the person, but in the photos, my almost 20 year old daughter who is an athlete and in fairly good shape is wearing a size XL. She never wears an XL! She's a medium in quality made apparel and a lot of times a large in cheaper Chinese made apparel. If that helps give you any idea for sizing?! These also have the panty sewn inside and it was comfortable as well. After the small sizing, I'd say the biggest thing I think people might have a problem with is it bunching up in front. Especially if you don't have the smooth, flat athletic body type that allows this swim bottom to lie flat in front and show off the side ruched look. For everyone else who either has a tummy pooch, rolls, muffin top, or a keg (vs. a 6 pack), this ruched look will only (possibly) accentuate what you have. And because the elastic band around the top is thin and not a nice wide band for those of us needing a little support or control, it too will only accentuate any rolls you have in that area (and because it runs small). If you're completely confident in yourself and don't mind what shows and how, good for you and go for it. But for the rest of us, maybe look at other bottoms. For this 55 year old aging athlete, I've tried several other styles like this before, but they had wider waistbands and for some reason were more flattering on than this bottom was on me.

For everyone else who either has a tummy pooch, rolls, muffin top, or a keg (vs. a 6 pack), this ruched look will only (possibly) accentuate what you have. And because the elastic band around the top is thin and not a nice wide band for those of us needing a little support or control, it too will only accentuate any rolls you have in that area (and because it runs small). If you're completely confident in yourself and don't mind what shows and how, good for you and go for it. But for the rest of us, maybe look at other bottoms. For this 55 year old aging athlete, I've tried several other styles like this before, but they had wider waistbands and for some reason were more flattering on than this bottom was on me.

The bottoms seem like they would work well for any body type. They do not cut into me on bottoms or top. Top is lined nicely and isn’t over done with wires and thick seams. It’s rather comfortable just wearing it.

UPDATE: I broke down and bought a better quality swimsuit (Sunsets) and am sending the suit I thought I would keep, back. There is a huge difference in quality, and I thought why on earth would I pay someone to tack down the dress and tighten up the straps? The better quality swimsuit beats this one any day. I bought this suit specifically because the description said it had underwire and the padding could be removed. Neither is true. There is a side wire, which is very but no true underwire. Nor does it come out, which I was ok with. I’m not ok with the padding because it does not come out! There is a slit in the bra as if it might be removable, however it is sewn in. The girls really don’t need that extra help. I would also benefit from adjustable straps. I bought a 2nd suit that I am going to keep that I will have to have altered to pull up the straps. As I was expecting, the part that connects the “shorts” to the suit is white and there is zero control; for me that was ok because the skirt did a good job covering up my pudge. The problem I foresee, especially with the black, is when I’m swimming and the skirt floats up, that’s a sight I don’t want anyone else to see! I am going to have the seamstress tack it to either side of the short portion to hold it down. 36DD(sometimes 3rd D) 165lbs and 5’6”. The other suit I bought was the Prussian blue color, which turned out to be more of an darkish teal green. I was sad about that, but I’m keeping it 1) because it was cheap and 2) I look halfway decent in it even though my boobs are front and center. Good luck. Don’t forget… you get what you paid for! Not terrible for sure but not quite what I wanted.

I'm 5'3 with all my height in my legs and the majority of my "fluff" in my belly. Currently I'm about 175 lbs. My typical sizes at the moment are 14 in bottoms and XL in tops. They are also a bit loose at the bottom of the legs. It's very minor and not a deal breaker, if I went a size down the shorts would work but the top would not. So the top fits just fine it has breathing room not to tight not to loose if I went a size down it would be to short and most likelyto tight based on how this one fits. I purchased this when it was a deal of the day so the pricing was really good and the very minor fitting issues aren't enough for me to send it back. I am not giving it the 4th star because I feel the sizing is just a little inconsistent enough that it's not just a me thing. I'd give it a 1/2 star for 3-1/2 total but that's not an option. Overall it seems like a good enough set to hangout in a pool.

ORIGINAL REVIEW: I am 5’1” and weigh around 180. I ordered an XL and the tags on the swim suit top and bottom said 14W (I normally wear a 16-18 on the bottom, 14-16 on the top, and carry a lot of weight in my hips and thighs). When I pulled the swimsuit out of the package, it looked so small and I thought there was no way it would fit. I was pleasantly surprised that it fit like a glove! I would purchase again as it is very comfortable.

This isn't the best swim top I own but it definitely isn't the worst. For the record, this is advertised as a plus sized swimsuit top and I don't particularly consider myself plus sized. BUT I am very busty. My overbust measurement is 41.5" and my underbust is 34", that said I wear a US 34H bra. For this reason alone, I considered that this may be a potentially decent fit and proceeded to order. I ordered the smallest size available and again, it's very nice. The fabric is soft, color is vibrant, is fully lined and has adjustable shoulder straps and a back strap. While the suit is very well made, there are a few issues I had. I understand the purpose of the foam inserts in the cup, but for a plus sized top, they were quite small. I fully believe this is meant for someone who may be a bit bigger around in the band and smaller in the cup. I found the band to be a bit loose on me and the space allotted to cover the bust just didn't seem like enough fabric. For those reasons, I had to cup the band underneath the girls to prevent underboob. The back, while partly adjustable, was difficult to hook. I had to do the "hook in front and twist around"method. I'll primarily be wearing this as support beneath my rash guard so that should be okay but just know that you definitely will want to pay attention to the size chart. I'm not saying it needs underwire but perhaps larger cups. I've seen swimsuits that have the foam support pads sewn into the suit and they make a world of a difference. A thicker support band would also be . For bras, the majority of the support comes from the band, straps and underwire or molded cups help work support and shaping. I think a wider band would add more support and security. Lastly, making the back band fully adjustable with either a hook and eye closure with multiple rows or have the slide hook closure it has now but allow for the full back to be tightened. Overall I think this is great for plus sized ladies who may have up to a DDD cup but any larger than that and you're in for some trouble.

I sorted through hundreds of bathing suits on Amazon before choosing this one, and it was a great choice!

I’ll talk about the bottoms first and then the top. I’m not sure if it shows up or not but, for reference, I am 5’2” and I weigh about 110 pounds. I bought the XS because I’d rather have tight bikini bottoms over loose bottoms for swimming. The bottoms are stretchy, so I’m glad I sized down. I don’t think they will come down in the water, but if I would have gone with a small I feel like they would because of how much stretch is there. Overall, the bottoms are perfect. Wouldn’t change a thing about them. As for the top, it’s a love/hate relationship. I wear a size 34b and the xs is tolerable, but its definitely snug around me. I like the look of the top, it’s cute overall. However, the amount of fabric they give you I can definitely see a possible nip slip happening. The coverage is more for the outer part of your boob than the inner if you get what I’m saying, so depending on where your nip sits, there’s a good chance it will make an appearance. I’m trying to consider my options here. I’ve thought about using my sewing skills to add a little more fabric so that it’s not a concern. But I’m not sure yet. Overall, I like more about this suit than hate, so I will be keeping it. For the price, I feel like it’s a cute suit and I can work around the one thing I don’t like and will possibly fix myself. I think this suit is worth taking a chance on and if you don’t like certain things with sizing just return for a smaller or bigger size.

I am a 28F or about 34" bust (usually buy swimsuits from Panache but they stopped carrying my band size) and ended up keeping the XS size (I bought both S and XS to compare). The size S and XS bottoms were not different in size but were very different in cut (see photo - the XS is on the right and the S is on the left). The size S and XS bottoms were not different in size but were very different in cut (see photo - the XS is on the right and the S is on the left). The S bottoms were shorter, had a thicker elastic waist band, and less ruching. The XS definitely cut higher on me and had a lot more ruching on the stomach. I wish the XS also had the thicker elastic waist band since that was more comfortable. Otherwise, they were essentially the same size. I am a 36" hip and they both fit just fine.

I love this rash guard. I was worried about the torso length because I am long so I got one size larger. It worked out great. Hips are a little large on me but that is easy to fix compared to a short torso. Fabric is fabulous (82% Nylon/18% Spandex), semi-shiny, super stretchy and feels great. The zipper is first rate and makes it easy to slip on for a quick dip in the pool after work. Zipper locks in place at any height. There is a built-in support strap at the under-bust and sewn in cups. This worked well for support for me (38C). Suit is fully lined in black. I like the seam design with over the bust seam (princess seam I think) plus the regular side seam. They give it a sleek look.Fit: I got the XXL and it fit well in the arm, upper torso and bust. I think if I would have got my normal XL it would have been small in the chest even though the 39 to 41.5 bust dimension is exactly what I would want. For reference I am 40/35/39, 6 feet tall and weigh 188 pounds. My girth is 69 inches. Even with the larger size the torso is none too long on me. I normally need things that have at least 30 inches from shoulders to the crotch for them to fit, 31 or 32 is better.Overall, well made suit that fits well at one size larger, looks great and is easy to wear.

My photos do not do the color of this BLACK swimsuit coverup justice. It is a true black color, but I had sunshine coming through my window which makes this look a bit washed out or gray in my photos. I ordered the Black in a Size 2XL. I am 5' 6" tall, weigh approximately 210-215 lbs., wear a 42D bra, and a Size 18 in pants. This fit me perfectly, but without a lot of extra room, so if you're a bit larger than I am, you may want to size up to the 3XL. The length in the front is a bit shorter in the front than in the back, though when you are wearing this, you cannot tell. It hits me about an inch or two above my knees, so I like the length of this. The lace detailing on the front is super cute and has ties to tie it up if you like. This is made from 100% Rayon, which can, in fact, shrink if not properly cared for. They recommend washing in cold water and NOT drying it in the dryer. You can use an iron set on low to help remove wrinkles. Overall, I am happy with the fit of this swimsuit coverup and think it's a great value at $24.95 (February 2024).

I am 5’3.75” tall & this does not touch the floor or drag, which is a huge plus for me. I can button down all buttons I would use, down to my belly. The material is a medium weight cotton and is soft enough it won’t irritate my skin. Buttons seem properly sewn on and fit snuggly in the button holes. This is definitely a keeper for me. Shipping was “weird”. It gave me an arrival date of June 15-30th. I don’t recall the purchase date. Tracking indicated it made it through customs and the label was printed on May 30th. No updates until yesterday, June 6th, saying it was out for delivery but I didn’t get the package until today, the 7th, yet tracking says it hasn’t been delivered yet. Weird. Regardless, I am happy I got it before I left for my vacation, and I love the product. Highly recommend.

To start off, I just want to say I am 12 weeks pregnant, 5'4", 176 lbs, 36B bra size pre-pregnancy (already need new bras), 34" waist, 44" hips, and I ordered a 2XL in the color Cyan. I read A LOT of reviews to figure out if I wanted this swimsuit and if it would be ideal for pregnant women. When I received it and tried it on, I wasn't sure if it'd fit, but it does! For the most part anyway. The shorts are just a little bit tight but not bad enough where I have to return it...it's still comfortable. Also this suit is probably made for bustier women, but I fit in there fine without cleavage showing. Since I'll be growing in that area as well due to pregnancy, it doesn't bother me. It has underwire, is padded, and even has a push up that you can take out if you want to! I'll be leaving mine in as it's more comfortable for me. The white spandex underneath the swimsuit for the belly is also very stretchable so I am sure there will be plenty of room for my growing bump in the summer months. The skirt comes to mid thigh for me. The swimsuit is overall very cute, flattering, and comfortable. Makes me feel confident enough to go swim! It also came with a cute pearl bracelet! It is too small for my wrist, but my little sister will love it. I will be posting photos along with my review so you can see what it looks like on me. My bathroom lighting sucks and the pics are a bit blurry but I will post many in different angles. The color Cyan is 100% like the picture in real life as on Amazon, even though it looks otherwise in my photos. On another note, I received two swimsuits when I ordered and paid for only one. I am sending the other one back tomorrow. The return shipping was free on my part. All I have to do is print the label and stick it on the box and bring it to the post office. It was overall very easy and was pleased with the return service I was provided.

This review is for the teal floral in size xl (10-12). I am 5'4, bust size 36C. 160lbs. My average swimsuit size is a 14-16. I am one of those people who never order clothes online, especially swimsuits. However, I am going on a cruise and I wanted to find a swimdress that was flattering but also had a skirt that covered my backside and my upper thighs. After a LOT of research I decided on Cocopear. I read that others had said to order a size smaller than you are used to but I couldn't fathom that I could fit in a 10-12 so I ordered a xl (10-12) and a xxl (14-16). The xxl fit but didn't fit snug and the cups were too big. The xl fit snug and held in my tummy plus the cups fit much better. The colors on my monitor show the suit to be a bluer teal than it really is in real life. . In one of the pictures you can see both the suits side by side and they are two different shades. The larger one is more of a pine green with blue undertones.Pro- Suit in constructed well and is a heavier fabric than some store bought suit. The straps although not adjustable are soft on the shoulders. It has attached shorts and are comfortable on the legs. The bra cups have removable padding and are underwired.Con- The only thing I don't care for is the bra cups are stiff. The first thing I thought of was, that from the inside it looks like a coconut bra. Perhaps after washing they will soften up. That being said that is enough to make me not like this suit. In fact I may purchase more. I have see other suits that look like Cocopear but the ratings on Cocopear are better and after purchasing Cocopear, I won't try the others.

**For reference : I’m 5’8, 190 lbs, D cup, African features (Butt, hips, thighs) a.k.a shape** **First impressions** First thing, not going to lie, had no idea that there are flamingos on the swimsuit. That’s the reason I took off one star ⭐️. I thought it was JUST flowers………Birds?? (wasn’t overly excited about that). But it’s still pretty, featuring one of my favorite colors…PINK. **After trying it on** If modesty is important to you, like it is for me……..This a great buy. As a plus size lady who carries her weight in the hips, thighs, butt and stomach. I REALLY like the way this swimsuit flatters my full-figuredness. I’m quite shy and self conscious but I felt like this suit covered everything I wanted covered, while still being cute and feminine. My chest wasn’t popping out, so it wasn’t so low cut that you could see what I had for breakfast. No bum-cheeks on display. The flowy skirt hides your “dinner rolls” and even with post-op swollen stomach, it still looks pretty good. I love that it’s a one-piece. So there’s no chance of accidentally brandishing my surgical scars for the world to see. The “shorts” part, is comfy and a good length (did I mention I’m shy and modest?? Lol). I got this suit for vacation so, I’ll update after, and tell you how it held up to chlorine and sea water. So far, I’d definitely recommend. In fact I may buy another one in a different color as my #2 swimsuit.

This review is for the Grace Karin Swimsuit with tummy control and ruffle v neck, xx-large, haze blue. My measurements are 38DD, 41” waist, 51” hips which means that my bikini top is normally an XL (sometimes a large) and my bottom is definitely a XXL. I’ve always worn bikini separates, in part to accommodate the different sizes. But I recently began a quest to find a cute and comfortable one-piece suit. This suit is gorgeous. The haze blue is a soft smokey color. The ruffled sleeve flows nicely and isn’t too poofy. The neck is a crisscross V that covers my chest comfortably without being high cut; I have no fear of falling out of the top. It has removable cups. The suit is double lined everywhere except for in the back and butt. There’s a slight ruching detail underneath the bust, which I like. I didn’t feel like this had tummy control but rather just that the fabric over the stomach was more substantial to help things look a little more smooth. That being said, I could see a little bit of an outline of the void that is my belly button through the suit when dry. It has very full backside coverage and the hips are low like a brief cut. I tried hiking up the bottom of the suit at the hips a little bit (to elongate my not-long legs) but the style didn’t allow for that. I love so much about this suit and want the fit to be perfect but *for me* it’s not. My bust is a little bit smaller (38DD, 43”) than the 44-47” listed in the size chart but it didn’t feel excessively roomy. But the bottom of the bust hit too low for me. Did I mention that I’ve breastfed 5 babies? I want that bottom support to be high. I’ve included a picture where I’m pointing to where I’d like the underbust band to hit. Overall this suit is gorgeous and well made- it doesn’t feel cheap at all. I think it’s priced very well for the quality and extra details that it has.

First of all, this actually goes long with the size chart. So, use your measurements and you’ll be fine. I’ve had a mastectomy with reconstruction in the last 8 months. So, I don’t need the support as much. However, I do need a cup with flexibility. This one doesn’t really offer it, because of the underwires. You can easily push the wires out and pull out the wires. If you are larger busted, you might consider taking the wires out too, so you can reposition the girls better. The overall fit is great. The fabric is soft and hugs the body just enough to where you don’t feel like your jiggling like jello. I’m not convinced about the under layer since it’s boy shorts. Those aren’t really thick thigh friendly. However, the skirt is long enough that it’s covered. I’m looking forward to wearing it on a cruise next month and I don’t have to return it for a larger size!!! For reference, I’m 5’11” and usually wear a XL/2X or a 16/18.

I'll explain... the quality of this thing is rather questionable. There's a little seam/overlay sewed in to the side of each breast of the tank top - they are crooked and one lays down while the other is more like a pocket (see photo). The tank is terribly wrinkled (hopefully it'll flatten out after I get it wet). The breast pads were too small, which caused some massive pooch-ing if you're a small chested gal like myself (32AA ; i.e. FLAT). I replaced the breast pads and now it just makes me look like I have something that I don't (see photo), so I guess that's better than an empty pooch? Anyhow. Another weird part is that the crotch bunches up (like directly beneath me) into a little bloop of material - not all of it - just a small portion. Maybe because I have twig-legs so I don't fill out the material entirely? Not sure. But it looks weird. I would show what I mean, but that's probably even weirder. Aside from those concerns though - I love this swimsuit. I'm a modest individual and unfortunately I'm stick thin, so finding a swimsuit that makes me feel confident but not look like a grandma is difficult. This covers my upper half when I want, or obviously can just be a bikini top when I want some sun. And the shorts cover everything but are still very cute and flattering. For reference, I am 5'6, 95lbs, and my measurements are 29-23-33... I'm wearing an XXS.

This swimsuit is adorable. Modest & covers everything it should, while still being stylish. Very comfortable fit. I ordered a size up from what I typically wear, as most clothing from the zon tends to run small. I wear XL but chose a 2X. I feel like it fits well on the bottom, but just a little big in the bust & stomach. I have a pronounced 🍑 & usually have to size up my swimsuit bottoms anyway & why I usually wear a 2 piece. My true size would have been fine. It doesn't ride up & stays in place. The skirt can be worn down or tied on the side, for a more fun look, without exposing too much. This does have a padded bra with an eleastic band for support. The pads can be removed from tiny pockets on the inside. I got this for an upcoming family vacation with inlaws & extended family. I wanted something that had full coverage but didn't make me feel older than I am or like I'm wearing an oversized trashbag 😆. This was the perfect choice. Made from quality thick material. Doesn't feel cheap. Ruching in the middle hides my mom pooch. Has tummy flattening properties but not so much it feels like I'm squeezing into shapeware. I only removed a star because for whatever reason the bust makes the girls look flat. I'm a C cup & usually have to find swimsuits that keep them tucked in, whereas this is just the opposite. Even with the pads, its like they're pushed down by the bra, instead of being supported & cupped. With the deep V neck, it looks weird not to have even the smallest amount of cleveage. Could just be because I sized up or maybe it's meant to be that way for modesty. Overall, I still think this is a great swimsuit. It's so comfortable that I imagine this will be my go-to suit for the summer.

I'm a 29yo mom of 3, I'm 5'8" and I fluctuate between 195-200lbs. I love to swim with my kids, but even though I hate my body I also hate the feeling of heavy loose swim fabric all around me in bigger one piece bathing suits with better coverage, so I'm always looking for 2 piece suits that hold my boobs in place. This one fits well, I ordered XL after measuring my bust according to the size chart provided and I did not size up or down I ordered the suggested size. I have not swam in it yet, I just received it and tried it on, but the fabric quality is good for the price and I feel comfortable in it and have good mobility. The bottoms were advertised as "high-waisted" but on my body they just feel like regular bottoms, maybe slightly higher than normal. I'm okay with them, the crotch section is wide enough to cover everything and feel secure. I will probably order this again in a different color if I decide I need 2.

Please don’t mind the back ground, remodeling. I am 5.6” tall and I weigh 280lbs and I wear a 48 DDD bra. (Maybe a little bigger in the tatas) This is a 3XL and it fits me great! I was super skeptical… figure it would do weird shiz like accent my belly bulge or runs up my crack when I walk… ya know? Well I wore it around the house for about 30 mins and I was very surprised. No wedgies and it stayed in place! The skirt meshing really hides those lady lumps and the top held my girls in pretty well. They do have padding inside the top which I had to straighten out and roll my fist on them, back and forth to hash out the wrinkles in the pads. It was easy. The material is super soft! Even the meshing. It’s pretty thick, not super thin material and the green color is nice! I am not a huge fan of green but this goes together. I definitely like the 2 tone. We shall see what this baby does when I hit the water! Good luck girls. 🍀

5’2 145-ish lbs. boob size at 36-38 DD-ish. I Ordered a large. This must be one of the most flattering 2 pieces for a short stout aging body changing (doing whatever it wants) & distributing weight all over the place. I have that “middle age spread” pretty bad. Clearly I’ll never have an attractive body. Too much abuse and repeated weight gain & loss over the years. My stomach is all jacked up from having kids. I lost my old hourglass shape that was always distinguished no matter how large or small I was (except when pregnant). I got saddle bags all day long. My upper arms are yuge. But even though I don’t have an attractive body, I will at least look presentable in a two piece. The lower portion comes up enough to hide my rumply, crepey lower abdomen. The top adds support, lift, and substance to visually supplement some needed volume & proportion in the upper body. It is the last stretch of winter in the northeast USA, the WORST time for me as I’m ghastly white and haven’t been outside burning calories in MONTHS but I have to go. Even the ties in the back (I wish I could take a picture)...If you have that backfat fold like kinda under your arms , if you tie the top it in the back just so...you can disguise it...or at least take some of focus off of its appearance. I have this trip to a place where it’s warm year round and most of the women there will probably have fit & athletic bodies because the world didn’t stop and leave them indoors like a reclusive for almost six months, so with this swimsuit, I am less mortified to go. I have not worn a 2 piece in probably over 10 years. Whoever thought up this design is a genius. Also, I may have been able to wear a medium because when I tie the ties, and it feels like I’m tieing all the way it can possibly go. There’s a chance a medium may even look more flattering than this, Maybe not. I am not sure. It’s always a little risky I think to try the smaller possibilities. I could only know for sure if I was able to try them both on. So if you are my size or a little smaller than me... know that you might be borderline the small side of a large also... maybe even a medium.

This is a really great bathing suit, with very nice and thick material! I don’t feel it’s too sheer in any way. The pattern and design are beautiful. The green is an amazing color green. I like the tie halter top, and I like how you can tie/adjust the other straps under the main hooking strap. The only thing for me was I feel I could have used a XL top, cup wise. The L bottoms fit so perfectly, I wouldn’t have wanted to size those up to XL. I tied the halter tight, and it will work and wasn’t uncomfortable since it’s just the cup part that seems snug. PROS: Full coverage bottom, cute pattern. CONS: Can't buy separate sizes/Cups big for my A-sized chest I'm gonna ride the wave of body positivity and post this out there for all my fellow Amazon shoppers. I read reviews and feel I need to do my duty and share. I have high expectations with a swimsuit and have been searching for a replacement for my five-year old old trusty black bikini. I swim at least once a week in the summer/take trips to the beach twice a year, and hot tub in the winter. I need a good swimsuit. I've been looking for three months now, both online and offline in economical price ranges. I found that what is the trending is to show the booty cheeks? I got ample roundness back there and can't stand to have much cheek showing. I feel naked! So I took a chance on this 'full coverage' bottom. First, let me say, I'm a pear. I'm always hesitant to buy when you can't pick separate sizes for top and bottom. In pants/dresses I wear size 10 bottoms, XL or L on yoga pants/shorts/bathing suit bottoms. And up top, I'm a med/or 36A. I followed the sizing guide, and got a medium. AND PRAYED. I have to say, the bottoms fit very nicely. I squish the front band down a little, as with it pulled all the way up, shortens my torso and makes me look a little chunkier. The top fits snugly around the chest(band), and the cups are a little big, but I won't be falling out, and the coverage is ok. I feel like I can swim laps, splash around, and even take this into the ocean and down waterslides without slippage or whoopsies. I was worried when I opened it up and saw that it was only lined on the front. But after a quick dunk in the pool, the fabric opaqueness holds up. This is the third swim suit I've ordered from amazon- and the only one I won't be returning. (for reference the other two were cupshe and angeleralla in same price range of $30). I just hope to lose a little weight up top, so that it's not so tight in the around the band.

Ordered a medium. 5'4", 145-150 pounds, athletic curvy build, wear clothes from sizes 6 to 12, usually 8 or 10. I love the bottoms - nice modest cut. Almost kept the suit just to wear the bottoms and find a more size-appropriate top. The bottoms fit in back very nicely with full coverage and have tons of extra fabric in the front which could accommodate a round belly. The top is great for a 32" band size overall, and the width of the straps and extra strap at back were hopeful for comfort. Out of the 32" around, there isn't have enough of that dimension across the FRONT for more than a a B to C cup size. The fabric across the front has no hope of covering D+ breasts, major spillage. I hope the picture helps - that panel to the side isn't to the outside of the cup (like it looks on the model) it's actually over the outer third of the cup. The top is just too small side-to-side in front for larger cup sizes, there isn't enough fabric there to rework to accommodate larger breasts. This would be a great suit if the tops and bottoms were sold separately, or the top came with cup sizes.

My build: I am boxy with a short torso 5’5 ft and am currently 130 lbs, with a solid/athletic build. I’m small chested: A 34 Aish-Bish, post kids. I’m still trying to learn my new body after children. I don’t want to knock this suit too much because some of my biggest problems really may just be unrealistic personal expectations and my specific build. I’m at a year and a half (ok, almost 2 years) out from having had my second bambino. Following that pregnancy, I have a delicious leftover herniated belly button, loose skin, 10 lbs that refuse to come off, a few stretch marks, and a mama bear crease. So ordering this suit was an attempt to basically have a glorified one piece that covered all that hot mess, maintained some modesty, and helped me stop looking like I was on my way to a swim meet in my mom-speedo. I typically wear a small now with some M and some XS depending on how the clothes run. I was a pretty solid size 0-2 XS and 32 A, before kids, so if you’re built similarly and, like me, are still struggling to figure out your new sizes and body after kiddos, this may help. I ordered the small. The top is big. My nibblets definitely do not fit the cups, but it’s made in such a way that no one would know, and it doesn’t gape open. The bottoms are my biggest issue (with my build). I feel like I may need medium bottoms. They’re a bit snug and are uncomfortably tight but are still workable-ish. But if anything, I feel like I need an XS top. So I’m a little perplexed on returning or not. Perhaps this will help my fellow boxy, small chested sisters. If I could go back, I probably wouldn’t have ordered this suit, and checked out one in store where I could mix sizes. I could see how with the right build, this could be a really great suit for some though 😊 Also, the material is not too thin. Good luck in deciding!

I'm 4'11", 112lbs and fairly petite. My chest is 36" and usually I wear a 30DD bra. I ordered the small and was quite pleased! I have a pouchy lower stomach and like that the ruching downplays that. It sits fairly high on me because I have a short torso but imagine it would hit at the bellybutton for a majority of other wearers. The colors are vibrant and lovely! I like the push up top as well - adds just enough sex appeal but still feels fairly full coverage. The material is thicker than I expected but if the quality ends up matching the price I won't be disappointed if I only get one season out of them.

I wear a size 18/20 and Xl, 1X, XXL, 2xl (100% cotton) in clothes. Some pants I'm in a size 16. This ran too small on me. I ordered based on the bust size of 42 inches. I have a small chest with a B cup, so finding swim wear is hard. This was too tight on me and it shown every bump and flab. Even the width around was tight without much stretch. I'm thinking even someone at a 42C would have a hard time getting the top on.

Holy cow this swimsuit is ULTRA comfortable!! I felt NO binding seams, or any seams at all for that matter. It truly is the most comfortable swimsuit I have ever worn. I did NOT find the "tummy control" to be the kind to pull you in at all, but rather the kind that is visually appealing because of the stylish folds that improves the appearance of the tummy. So I would call this tummy flattering, but not tummy control. The pictures I included are the model wearing a suit that is XL, whereas size L would have been a better fit but the XL still does fit nicely, so I think the size is forgiving both up and down but I recommend you choose down a size. I love that it did not dig into me anywhere, and also that it is super soft everywhere. The lace around the neck line is not itchy at all. You dont feel it at all. The breasts are hugged, not squashed in this suit! The back of the suit has a flattering scoop and flattering cut on the bottom. i definitely recommend this suit!

Let me start by saying WOW! I love it! I was worried about several things when buying a bathing suit online 1. Would my boobs be flopping allover the place? I am a 34F 2. Would the bottoms actually be high enough on my torso to be over my belly button and cover my loose skin from having gigantic twins? 3. Would the straps around the back fall in just the right place to accentuate my back fat? Short answers: -Amazing breast support with structured cups and adjustable back. (If your boobs are down to your belly button a bathing suit won’t support them period, expecting that isn’t fair) that being said I accept my sag. -Bottom is high enough that it covers exactly what I want covered (and my saggy skin is way higher up than other moms because I had two seven lb babies at one time, stretched me to the max!). - The back straps fall in the perfect place to HIDE my back rolls!!!!! Full butt coverage. I would say the material is a 6-7 on a scale of 10 but for the price it exceeds expectations. For reference I’m 5’3”, 157lbs, size 8-10 pants, size L underwear and pants , I’ve had three children. I am a 40” chest, 34 waist, 42.5” hip. I read the reviews and size chart and decided to go with the XL. I’ve never been and XL in anything but I am very happy with the sizing. It’s not tight so I don’t look like exploding biscuits. I think once wet it may be semi loose but not so much that I would have sized down. I’ve never felt sexy again after having kids. This suit helps me feel sexier and more comfortable walking around in a bathing suit.

I ordered this bikini as soon as I saw “size 2-10” on the small-medium. I was intrigued and really hopeful that I had found what was sure to be the most comfortable and perfect fit on the market. I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 Jean and small or medium bikinis. I have extra wide hips and struggle to find swimsuit bottoms that don’t ride up since my booty is upside-down heart shaped. It is fascinating how much stretch this bikini has. Truly! I really like that the stretch allows the sides of the bottoms to be high or low on the hips. I love it. This bikini is light coverage with big comfort. Perfect for a day of lounging or light water use. Not the most flattering top for my chest because it does give the pinched boob look due to no boning, lift, or padding. But again, super comfortable, like wearing a thin bralette.

I'm 5/4" and 155 lbs, and I usually wear a size Medium top and Medium (or occasionally Large) on the bottom. I went with Amazon's recommendation to get a Large in this swimsuit, but it's still too small. I extended the adjustable straps all the way and removed the bra pads, but it's still so tight that my back bulges out over the top. Because of the tightness, it's difficult to pull on and take off. On the plus side, this is a cute suit with a retro feeling. It isn't cut too high in the legs or too low at the top, which I appreciate. The fabric is very nice, soft with a ribbed look on the outside and smooth inside. There's a safety seal over the crotch area that is easily removed. I washed it in cool water on gentle cycle and line dried it, and it came out just fine. It might fit me just fine if I happen to lose five pounds, but I can't comfortably wear it now.

(Please disregard my children's fingerprints in the pictures :-) ) First, let me say that these are very comfortable and fit very well overall. (reviewing from my bro’s account) — i’m so bummed!! i wanted to love these pants. ordered a L after reading reviews; i love baggy clothes but didn’t want to swim in them, so i went with my true size. i got the ones with the pleats, maybe that’s where i went wrong — they’re roomy in the leg like i wanted but the crotch is cut really weird. they’re also pleated in the back. doesn’t matter how/where i placed them, it did that thing where it looks like your thighs are eating the pants. if i dropped them to sit lower on my hips, they rise on my bum. good length (5’2”, they’re at my ankle). the material is soft, didn’t get “scratchy” from them. definitely a blended fabric; not heavy or super light, but you can get away with them in like, 70 degree weather. if you have thighs, hips, and a bum, i do not recommend; at least the pair with pleats. i may cross my fingers and try the regular, loose pair!

(sorry for the blurry mirror) For reference I’m 5’4” 135lbs and a 31 year old mom. I ordered these first in size 4/27 (my normal Levi’s size) but they were too big. I re-ordered in size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. I am normally not a size 2/26 so they ran large on me. They are extremely flattering on my pear shape. NO gap in the back of the waist! I have a thick bottom half (and short legs) and I don’t like to wear short shorts. These fit really nice! Nice mid to high rise on them. You can see in my picture it comes to about my belly button. You don’t have to worry about bending over and showing plumbers butt. The dark wash is really nice and classy looking. The pocket placement is great. They are overall extremely flattering. A little bit of stretch, but not too much. Pictures taken when the shorts were new but they’ve been washed and worn since with no issues to the actual shorts. My only issue was with the denim wash. I did have to use a color catcher while washing. It still dyed one of my white hand towels light blue! Be careful of color transfer with the dark wash! The only thing I wish was the bottom leg holes of the shorts was a tiny bit smaller so it hugged my knees a little tighter. I’m just picky! The bottom is not sewn into place so you have to roll the shorts when you get them out of the dryer. They are really flattering on the backside and I definitely recommend them for pear shapes like me. I love not having to pull shorts down from riding up when I walk. That is why I like bike shorts and Bermuda shorts. If you have big thighs you know what I mean. I think curvy girls in particular will love these. The quality on mine is very good! I am definitely going to stock up and order a bunch for summer. I wish they had more colors in my size. Hopefully this review was to someone out there!

[Height: 5'5| 155lbs | Bust 37 | Waist 27 | Hips 37] [Ordered: Medium] I can never tell how long a pair of pants will be on me based on the stock pictures, and often some reviews of those in a similar height [weight can be carried differently]. In this case, these pants are short. The inseam is 27" on these, and I prefer an inseam of 32" or longer (depending on the shoes). However, there is a hem that can be removed to increase the length of the pants. Overall, they are cute and comfortable to wear!

* For reference, I am 5 foot two, approximately 115 pounds. The size medium hits me right at the ankle bone, and is a very comfortable fit all the way around. * The fabric has no stretch, but the pants are loose enough that they are still very comfortable. * there are three front pockets, and two back pockets. * there is elastic at the waist only in the back. BOTTOM LINE: cute, comfy, stylish!

**Update: My fiance actually purchased these for me for Christmas and he had ordered me the Medium and truthfully, the medium fits so much better and the legs still aren't baggy like I was worried about in the first place. These definitely fit more true to size than the Levi maternity shorts do! If you're between sizes I would definitely say you're safe sizing up. I'm 5'4" and pre-pregnancy I weighed about 133 and was wearing either size 6 or 8 AE jeans or 27 in Miss Me and chose to try these in size Small because I had ordered Levi maternity shorts for my last pregnancy and found that even a Small in those ran a little big and I'm glad I went with Small on these instead of Medium. I'm currently 22 weeks pregnant and now up to about 147 and I'm afraid Medium would have been too big and I'm not one for wearing super tight jeans or leggings. I will admit the legs are a little bit tighter than I like but they are stretchy and I still feel like I have room to grow. I'm carrying low and the band snaps under my belly but I'm at a point I can no longer button my normal jeans so these work well and I feel like I should be able to wear these throughout most of the rest of my pregnancy. I would definitely recommend these and if you're on the fence between sizes go for the smaller size otherwise you may end up with baggy "skinny" jeans.

The back pockets do fit high because they are high waisted (if you care about that) I don’t because they fit everywhere else. Usually when I find pants they fit right on my waist but loose on my legs or if they fit right on my legs they are WAY too tight around my belly. These stretch perfectly to fit all areas. ✅Likes: ○ The color/wash is not too bad. Although I hate the fit of these (more to follow) but the wash is better than some other barrel cheap jeans I've gotten. No weird yellow tone or corny/exaggerated y2k fades. Kind of leans acid wash though so I don't like that as much though, but at least it's pretty even-toned. ❌Dislikes: ● Insanely short. If you're looking for the "barrel jeans via CAPRI" these may be up your alley, more gen-x/millenial does trendy than the ACTUAL barrel jean trend which bows out more and is BAGGY+full length. And no, I'm not tall. In fact, I'm barely average, a mere ~5'2" with short legs so... My previous go-to barrel jeans are a flat 26.5" in all sizes and that's MUCH better IMO... I ordered a 6 but tbh if I knew these were supposed to fit capri length I would try a maximum size 14 just so they sat lower (my reg size is M 26" waist, 39" hips) ● Not just short/capri style but they are AT LEAST AN INCH SHORTER THAN THE CHART so all I can say is size up IMO! 0.5-0.7" variation was incorrect in my case, tba if it's an accident but I think you should expect 1"+ so size up. ● Cheap construction, SUPER cheap zipper, 4 odd thick felt patches inside, super tight button hole, thick (but hollow) button, uncomfortable plastic thread ends everywhere, slippery synthetic pockets, etc ● Says "non stretch" yet there is spandex so yeah, they're somewhat stretchy and elastic will wear down

10/10, absolutely LOVE these. I’ve tried MANY barrel jeans on Amazon at this point and I think these are my fav so far. Primarily because they have some stretch and are the perfect length! Stoked to wear these! —an additional bonus is that they came quickly, within a couple days. They didn’t have any odd smells to them, so I am super happy! Don’t question it, get them! They’re the best. I wear them ALL the time. They’re my new everyday jean. They’re sooo comfy but so flattering. I did have to have my mom sew and fix the pockets so the lining didn’t keep showing, but it was an easy fix and they never show anymore!

2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

These have just the right amount of high rise. The dark wash, straight leg and positioning of the back pockets all do me favors. I will have to hem these as I do with almost all pants and will not be rolling the ankle because the inner color of fabric is light. Rolling the cuff will create a light band at the bottom that detracts from the elongating effect. Fabric does feel nice and soft yet sturdy. It’s not stiff and there is a slight stretch to accommodate ease of movement. Seams are all well done and pockets seem well reinforced. I expect these to last me many years. Amazon fashion purchases are always hit and miss, but if you are disciplined about returning items you don’t love, true bargains can be had. This is definitely a score despite the color bleeding.

I took photos without a shirt for transparency of how they fit on someone thick waisted like myself. With a shirt you wouldn't be able to detect the waistline. The slimming tummy panel hides any fat rippling. These are jean material and have the same weight and durability as a regular pair of jeans versus jeggings. Once I lose weight I will size down as the tighter my midsection gets then the slimming tummy is more effective.

5’3” 29” waist ~145lbs Fabric : Nice drapey linen blend(?) fabric does well in heat and humidity, and has a flattering flow to it. Fit: has a real draw string, with no hidden elastic waist, so they are easily adjustable. I ordered the XL, for a drapier fit, and they did shrink a bit in length after the first cool wash. Definitely wash on cold to avoid shrinkage. Color & Transparency: Color is as pictured, slightly transparent but wearably so- i have tattoos on my legs and bum and if pulled tight you can see them, but for normal wear they’re just fine. You’re not seeing right through them. Overall very versatile, good for hot and humid weather, comfortable and flowy, and look really put together with monochromatic looks. Can be dressed up or down a bit depending on what you pair with them. I’d say they’re a good value.

5’7” 195- 200lbs Size 18 is too big because I thought the regular size 16 would be too small. Should have bought regular size. I did not like the length. I know they are supposed to be at the ankle. They can be rolled up to look stylish. This is great for business casual days at work and for nurses who wear regular clothes but still need to do patient care. Nice to not burst out of pants while bending. I purchased mine too large. I was worried that the belly would be too tight. The elastine lets the waist stretch without being granny style elastic. I recommend offering your actual size. I would buy again. I will keep these because they are not jeggings and can still be worn baggy style. I recommend a figure defining top to give you a shape because the pants are baggy. I will keep these size 18 and order another pair sized to my regular size 16 and then style like the model. The first pair will become lounge jeans and work around the house jeans because they really are comfortable without being jeggings. I recommend them but - let the model photos be your guide to styling.

A bit mad I so hastily took off the tag on these, but my main issue is that they keep sliding down! I’m 25 weeks, 5’6, 190 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually a size 10-12 with 41-42 inch hip, so I ordered a large. Having worn under-the-bump jeans and finding that they were hitting in an uncomfortable spot, I wanted a pair of pants that go over the bump with front pockets so I can hook my work badge. Maternity pants with front pockets appear to be a rarity. These pants keep slipping down! The legs feel fine, although they run long so I need to roll/cuff them, but the waist is not staying in place. I feel like the bottom of the panel is constantly falling down to my crotch instead of staying above it. There’s no belt loops either, so it’s awkward to try to “hike up” the pants constantly. I’m not sure if sizing down would work, given that the legs are perfectly fine for me in the large, but these pants were not the 10/10 I was hoping for. Maybe they’re best for mid to late third trimester. Included a pic when they are first hiked up and another once I’ve been walking around to show the slump!

A nice fitting pair of jeans, that are comfy are an investment for me. I'm all about quality, fit and comfort with jeans. I'll pay a higher price if need be. I'm somewhat hard to fit and picky when it comes to jeans. These were cute and the reviews were convincing. I'm a true size 4 in jeans and pants. 27 inch waist. I have narrow hips and pants usually creep down on me and I have to crank them as the day goes on, if I don't have a skinny belt on. These have an elastic band, and they feel like the'll stay "put." LOL Pay close attention when you read their sizing chart. Make sure your'e reading the correct chart for sizing. Either skinnys or boot cut, because both charts are different sizing. The first pair I ordered was a size 2 28' waist. Too small! Returned those and ordered a size 4 29" waist and those fit. I received the second pair promptly. I ordered 32" inseam. So, the manufacturer tag is marked 4L/waist 27 L32. Again, this varies from the size chart listed on the add. Other than the confusing sizing, they are cute,fit nice and comfy. The waist is elastic but isn't "overly" stretchy though. Somewhat light weight compared to other jeans, only moderate amount of stretch. Hopefully they won't get too baggy and stretched out the longer the wear time.

A pant that finally lives up to the title of WIDE LEG! These definitely are wide and flowy!!! The waist comes in nicely and if it's a little large, the tie helps to secure it in place. The material is soft, silk-y like in my opinion. It's not scratchy or see-through, though they are light weight. I love this type of pant for my body style - big thighs small waist. You'll want to be strategic with what you wear on top, since the bottom does have a lot of extra material. I wonder if I could have sized down one size to get rid of a little extra fabric... overall, very pleased! This will be a perfect summer/spring pant and something I'll wear to work. For reference: I'm 5'5" and weigh roughly 155lbs. I normally wear M on top, L on bottom. This is a size L and I may have been fine with a M.

absolutely adore these pants. 5’6 200lbs pear shaped wearing the XL. i have them in grey, green, and black. be mindful of what style you are getting to ensure it is the fit you want, all of mine are the original style. the three i have have are pretty damn consistent in fit and length but the waistband is just slightly looser on the grey than it is on the other two, after a few wears they all begin to feel the same. the material is not see through, it is a very sturdy and borderline heavy fabric but still light enough for me to feel comfortable wearing them throughout the summer in 100+ degree weather. they are so comfortable and flattering, but i think your height does matter for them to fit you correctly. they ride right on my belly button for my ideal length, i think people who are taller or have longer legs than me would need to wear them in a low rise fashion to prevent a high waters look. shorter people can definitely just opt for a higher waist or hem them to make it work. i wash them on delicate and hang them up to dry, i do not want to risk destroying them in the drier. i’m super happy with these pants and wear them most days. i’ve only had them during summer months but you could definitely wear them with leggings underneath for added warmth in the winter.
