According to Amazon's recommendations people who order the clothing sizes I do order these in a large. According to the size chart I would wear a large. I am 5'2" and weigh 160lbs. I ordered a medium because reviewers said these ran large. Medium is WAY too big. I'm not even convinced that a Small would be the right size. These are about 6" too long and are so baggy that they remind me of something from the 90s back when we wore pants big enough to fit two people inside of. I feel like maybe these were produced using a men's last and mislabled as for women. These are awful, they are just so large, and the polyester fabric is slightly shiny, making them feel like a high school athletic uniform in the weirdest way. The only bright side is that because they have so much polyester in them they are incredibly warm and don't breathe, so if you're somewhere cold these will keep you warm. But maybe order 2-3 sizes down.

According to the Amazon Size chart a waist of of 28 inches is a size 2. I am 5'3 and 122 lbs, and I did use a tape measure to determine my size. When I received these pants, the size on them says 26x28. I chose a size 27 length. So obviously the size chart provided isn't correct. Hopefully they will get the size chart corrected. (I do plan on notifying them of it.) If not, I would recommend ordering a size up. Luckily these jeans do still fit me, but they are a little more tight than I would like, and I have a little bit of a muffin top with them. I went with the 27 inch length which I think looks cute, but if you want the jeans to go to your shoes I would go with a longer cut. I'm short so it's rare for me to find pants that don't sag to the ground. When wearing a shirt that isn't tucked in, the muffin top isn't visible. I ordered 2 pair of these jeans in different colors, but I might return one for a size up.

According to the invoice I ordered XL. I was sent 2XL and it’s gigantic in the shoulders. I was hoping to exchange for a L but the return process did not give that option. The jacket was soft and distressed. It was a little bit odd that there was a Picone in the front pocket, but I assume maybe that’s what they used to distress it. It looks like it would’ve been better to order True size. I am 5’3 and 180 pounds, and I should’ve gotten a large. The denim is very soft.

According to the size chart measurements I decided that a size 16 would be the right one for me. These actually have a lot of stretch in them, so I maybe could have gone for a 14 instead as I do occasionally have to hitch these pants up again, lol. Not the fault of the pants though! They are made of 71% cotton, as stated on the product page and on the tag inside the jeans, so they may shrink when I wash and dry them, and that will help with the slipping down. They have four functioning pockets, two front with flaps that snap closed and two back pockets without flaps. They are somewhat short, so that makes them great for my height (5' 4") and under, I think. Even on me, they just barely get to my ankles. The material is sort of thick, so I think they will be nicely warm in the winter. I have worn them a couple times with temperatures around 70 F and usually I am happy to get home and put something cooler on. I love the color of them and the comfy fit. With all the stretch they have, there is no binding or pinching, no matter what direction you can contort your legs into. They are well made and all around just a nice pair of jeans, especially for shorter people. I definitely can recommend this pair of jeans. Comfort and fit are great with them.

I would say these are perfect for casual Sunday afternoons. They look just a tad too much like pajamas (in my opinion) to wear out to run errands or meet friends for coffee. I love the color, I love the flowing look, I love the feel of the fabric. This is a nice, good quality, casual pant that is well crafted and well designed.

As an elder millennial, I'm super stuck on the skinny jeans trend and having trouble getting into the straight leg style that everyone is into right now. However, I'm a huge fan of Hudson jeans, specifically the Nico style, so I was really excited to try these. And I do like them more than expected with a few caveats. They definitely run a little big when compared to other Nico jeans I have. I have several pairs of Nico Super Skinny jeans in size 25 that fit perfectly, so that's what I got these pants in, too. And while they fit OK, they're definitely large in the waist and I think I could have comfortably sized down. I'm 5'3, 100 lbs for reference. As you can probably see from my photos and the listing photos, the jeans are also quite a bit longer on me than on the model. Just a couple things to keep in mind when you're trying to decide what size to purchase! I'm still trying to feel totally comfortable in a straight leg jean, but these are helping me get there- they're super comfortable and relaxed (much more so than skinny jeans!) and they feel like a high-quality jean. If you're a fan of Hudson and looking for a good straight leg jean, I definitely recommend these... But you may want to size down from your other Hudsons!

As I (begrudgingly) get older, I struggle with trying to remain fashionable, but also dress my age. Shorts can be tough for me because I already don't love wearing them and there are so many super short, uncomfortable pairs that seem to let everything hang out. I took a gamble on these as I wasn't exactly sure on my size or how they would look. I'm 5'3 and 100 lbs, and usually wear either 25 or 26 in jeans and I opted for the size 2s in these. They ended up being absolutely perfect! They fit me super well- they have a fair amount of stretch, but they're not too snug around my waist and are incredibly comfortable. I also love the length of them. They're not super long (a look I don't love), but are a "respectable" length. I feel like they're trendy, but I also feel comfortable going out and about in these and that I look my age (almost 40), but a cool version of it. I love the wash and it will go with so many different things. It's easy to tuck shirts into the shorts or leave them out. The price on these is great and I'll definitely be getting a lot of wear out of them this summer!

As most people who shop for various products online , I was a bit hesitant to buy jeans sight unseen. Some things you just HAVE to try on. These babies for great right out of the box! The rise is perfect (about an inch below bellybutton) and they aren’t too snug in the hips or thigh area. (Gotta love stretch denim👍🏻) But most importantly, the length/inseam was spot-on for what I like. I am almost 6 feet tall and around 170lbs and can fit anywhere between a 9 and 13, depending on the cut and brand. They look great with boots, tennis shoes, AND flats! I have the hardest time finding a jean that looks good on my frame without trying to dress like a teenager and I try to avoid the horrid “mom jeans” at all costs. (I’m 48). After a washing they still fit great. They get just a bit loose around the waist at the end of the day (as most jeans do) but a decent belt solves that problem easily. I will definitely be snatching up the other cuts and colors from Wallflower! I have had a compliment from every person I’ve seen that knows me in the last few days, even one person that asked me if I had lost weight. Nope! Just a SUPER cute, awesome fitting pair of jeans! I feel like my wardrobe has expanded now that I can wear shirts that I haven’t been wearing because I feel uncomfortable showing off the ol’ muffin top. Problem solved! Thanks Wallflower for catering to the real woman, and not just the teenie bopper stick figure... definitely one extra satisfied customer here!

As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

As w/ seemingly many others, I bought my first pairs @ CVS (got 3). I don’t normally shop for clothes there, but they were a serendipitous discovery, and having a hard-to-fit body where I don’t often find items that I can wear off-the-rack (I’m an endomorph, or “apple” type, where my waist is short & thick), I wanted to look for more pairs, solids specifically (black, heather grey & indigo w/ gold threading), to have as wardrobe basics. I was THRILLED to find them on Amazon (and they do have their own website, as well), and bought the pairs that I was looking for, after reading reviews from customers who ended up becoming Lildy fans in the same manner. These jeggings are SO comfy that you can literally wear them 24/7, and even for activities that require a broad range of movement (splits, high kicks). I wear them to sleep, because I’m at that stage in my life where I’m too lazy to change in & out of clothes constantly (just turned 47). I have a “uniform” that works for me (wifebeater & jeggings), and it’s nice to be able to simply add accessories, outerwear & shoes, and voila….I’m dressed to go outside. 👌 Prior to finding the Lildy jeggings @ CVS, I was wearing leggings, but would wear a mini over them solely for the coverage to hide the cameltoe. Now, I don’t have to do that because the material for the jeggings is thicker. I save myself from needing to put on an extra garment that constantly shifts from movement, and requires additional labor to adjust & launder. Dressing is much easier, and I effortlessly look pulled together. I always wore bootcut or flared pants (they were more flattering for my silhouette), but these jeggings fit so well that I feel confident about how I look in them (can’t say the same for the ones from Walmart), and unlike w/ flared or bootcut bottoms, these tuck into knee-hi boots w/o any of that extra fabric, and make looking cute such a non-job. Highly recommended, if you feel that the cut will work for your body shape. Great value for the price—you’ll get hooked & end up coming back for more colors & patterns. (For reference, I’m just under 5’2”, ~110 lbs., 28” waist & 28” inseam.) UPDATE: (May 2022) I’m now on my SEVENTH pair of Lildy jeggings! 👖 Just got the olive green solid color. Made just like the rest. I LIVE in these—you can exercise & sleep in these jeggings, and it’s nice that they look like “real pants.” 😆 The grey ones look fairly refined, almost like twill pants, and you just pull’em up like they’re you’re favorite pair of leggings, but might be fine for a “casual nice” work dress code. 🥰

They are the perfect garment to take on trips too, because they barely wrinkle. They are high waisted and run true to size and are of good quality, especially for the price!

At 5’7”tall these are more like clam digger length on me rather than capris length. I’m also not in love with the fit because while the waist fits me perfectly (for reference 127 lbs 27” waist ordered size 4) there’s some rather odd bunching going on in the hip, leg and butt area that’s not shown in the model photos and the fit is somewhat a little tighter in the calf area. Material wise, I got the dark blue and although the material is pretty nice, not too thin or too thick, I’m not really liking the color too much, makes them look kind of cheap and again, doesn’t look like the same color as shown on the model. The description also lists these as mid rise but they are actually high rise and hit me just slightly below my belly button, not necessarily a bad thing if you prefer high rise but if you’re looking for mid rise, keep looking. What I liked, good stretch (no need to size up - if anything size down one, especially if your measurements fall in between the sizes noted in the sizing guide) , nice deep front pockets and they are comfortable and seem pretty durable. Bottom line: 3 stars mainly because the listing description and seller photos are somewhat misleading. These jeans are just okay. I’ll still wear from time to time but for casual outings and outdoor activities only.

Authentic review incoming! I hope it's . My BFF, age 16, 1999: "What are you going to wear out tonight?" Me, age 16, 1999: "Well, black pants, obviously..." I wear leggings and joggers daily as an ultramarathoner and otherwise very lazy person, but haven't ventured back into flare/wide black pants since literally high school. I gambled on these and they're soooo comfy and cute. They're high-waisted, forgiving, with a wide waistband, nice deep pockets, and I love that they're not an aggressive flare; they're actually more like wide leg pants (or at least they are on me). One thing about the sizing: I'm 5'6" and 135-140 pounds but I'm built like SpongeBob SquarePants so I tend to size up to avoid things being tight in the waist. I can be anywhere from a small to a large depending on the brand. I went with a medium on this one and was hoping it wouldn't be too tight. Umm, quite the opposite. These are big on me, even in the waist (which is no small feat, pun intended), and even though I got the 30" at 5'6", they puddle on the floor a bit as shown in the picture. I don't mind because they are comfy AF and super cute. Except the time the hem got snagged in my rowing machine. Okay, so these will be more like around-the-house pants. Better yet, they'll be my pizza pants (shout-out to Joey Tribbiani). As if I haven't dated myself enough with this review, I can't help but think that these are giving JNCO (IYKYK). I hope I haven't scared off the trendy young'uns from buying these. I promise not to wear them out in public to embarrass you if you promise to stay off my lawn!

Awesome 70's vibe. I am about 5'5", 128 lb.. but very hip-py. I ordered the small, and I find them a little more snug than I prefer. So I am going to try a medium. They seem a little tight in the hips, and they're oddly tight at the knees. They're a little higher rise than I would prefer. I firmly prefer low-rise pants. These come right up underneath my belly button. Probably if they weren't so high-rise I would keep the small. They are very stretchy. I feel like I have short legs for my body, and with my shoes on, the length comes to about a 1/2 inch from the ground. My inseam is 30 inch. UPDATE: I ordered the medium and they were just too big. They fit better in the hips and the waist, but they were oddly baggy in the crotch. It looked like I was trying to smuggle the crown jewels in there. Also the inseam was about two to three inches longer on the medium. They were dragging on the floor. These are really super cute pants, but sadly I'm going to have to send both sizes back. I wish they had numbered sizes.

Based off the size chart I ordered the Medium. When I opened them, the waist looked tiny, but these pants are like magic. They stretch and are still comfortable and flattering. Like most flares, they run a bit long for me at 5'5 but look great with a taller shoe. Love the vibrant color too.

Based on other buyer reviews, I decided to purchase these in a size large. I'm 5'6 and 170 lbs. Typically I wear jeans that have a low rise because they're a better fit for my body type (extra weight in the tummy area.) High and mid-rise pants tend to cut into my belly fat and it creates these very unsightly lumps on my sides. Getting to the point, I wish I had gone with the medium instead of the large. They're form fitting but in a looser way. I like how they fit throughout the legs but I would prefer them a tad more fitting. Most jeggings (even the super stretchy ones) are too tight in the calves and ankles for me to wear comfortably. These are nice, smooth denim and very, very stretchy. They're perfect for someone like me who usually has trouble fitting into jeggings. There is a super comfy waistband that is equally stretchy (FYI: no belt loops or front pockets on these pants.) It's a higher waist but they're really easy to roll down, which is what I prefer. Even with them rolled down the waistband is still very big on me. I haven't washed them yet so I really hope they shrink some. If I were judging these on comfort level alone they would absolutely get 5 stars! They are literally just as comfortable as cotton leggings. The denim is very soft, not thick and rough like traditional jeans. I'm definitely ordering another pair in a smaller size! Highly recommend!!

Because I do not usually spend this kind of money on clothing and am pretty unreliable with completing online returns, I read every review I could find on multiple websites before I selected my size. I still got it wrong (lol). I don’t usually leave reviews, but there is a LOT of conflicting information about the sizing of these jeans out there and I want to share what I found to be true.When selecting your size, I recommend taking two factors into consideration:1. What is your body shape/type – i.e. where do you carry your weight? If you are someone with more of an hourglass shape or more thick in the thighs/butt, I would follow the guidance of most of the reviews and size up. If you are someone like me with a small/medium butt and legs, I would recommend selecting either your normal size, or one size down.2. How do you want the jeans to fit – i.e. do you want a relaxed fit or a tighter fit? For reference, I am around 5’7, 122 pounds, and usually a size 26/size 2 in pants. The size 26 Wedgies fit me perfectly right out of the bag. However, based on other reviewers’ experience, 100% cotton jeans will relax/mold to your body over time and use. If I kept the size 26, they would have evolved to more of a “boyfriend” style relaxed fit on me. I personally wanted a snugger fit. I then ordered the size 25. They were tight on me and not the most comfortable right out of the bag (they also made my butt look smaller which was the opposite of what I was hoping). However, after wearing them for a few hours, especially sitting in them, they relaxed and fit me exactly the way I envisioned.I attached pictures of me in both and indicated the size I am wearing in each photo. This is what each pair looked like immediately after opening the package - I did not take a photo of the size 25 after they relaxed, but trust me when I say, they're worth it! Hope this helps someone!

Being in my late 50's, it's tough to find a decent pair of shorts that are too long, too short, too high waisted...it's always something not quite right. Definitely found it with these shorts! Perfect length, actually fits comfortably, the material is also a bit stretchy, and the material is just thick enough to be tough without being too hot.I'm 5'9" and usually wear a size 4 or 6 depending on the cut. Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

Bitttccch (you're not a bitch, I'm just using it as a expression) if you haven't bought these pants then you need to. Now I've always had a problem with finding my size-- I always bought too big or too small. I am 5"5, 194 lbs w/ the dimensions of 33-42-25 (waist-hip-thigh). I ordered the size 14 and it fits me snugly, however not tightly bc its got enough stretch for you to pop a very wide street squat, do a high kick, etc. They are long so you can roll em up but that's ok. They are soft material and they don't feel restricting. You should know your measurements before buying if you are not sure what size in jeans you are, you can even ask if you aren't sure what size to get. But if not, try going with your normal size

Black and white so rich. This reasonably priced pant is super light weight, flows and comfortable. The pattern was well printed on the fabric. I felt very stylish. I'm 5'4" and wore a 1" espadrille and it was not dragging. I will get a lot of wear out of these pants. Purchased a L as recommended by Amazon. I'm usually a 10-12 jean. I felt like maybe I could be a M but the generous size made more comfortable and flows. So all good.

Black extra small xs fits me 5'2" 104lbs currently, with 25in waist(for high rise pants only). Inseam is roughly 28.5in which is why I must wear 4 inch heels with these pants. I do have long torso and short legs though. They are definitely more bell bottoms than flare pants. I never had a pair of bell bottoms before so when I first tried these pants on I wasn't sold on not being able to see my feet at all. I had to Google and found out it's normal to not see much of your shoes if you're wearing bell bottoms or wide legged pants lol As I tried more tops with these pants, I started to like them. These are truly high-waisted and have a very flattering cut. The fabric is relatively thinner but drapes very well. I don't think it would wrinkle easily. Think spring and summer pants. I tried out different tops with the pants since I never had bell bottoms before, and I found that bodysuits and crop tops probably work the best.Note there are only three belt loops in total.I ended up liking these pants a lot. Sexy and slimming and gives the illusion of longer legs. 4 stars instead of 5 because left pant leg is about 1/5 inch (5mm) longer than right pant leg and ever so slightly bigger leg opening.

Bummed these didn't work. I am 5'10", 170lbs and ordered the 10 Long. They are way too short for my taste. I'm fine with ankle length with a skinny pair of jeans but expect my boot/flair to at least touch the ground barefoot or with flats. I couldn't find the inseam length in the description when ordering but it turns out they are 32". I'm accustomed to "Regular" length being 32", "Long" at 34". Quality is really nice. I was worried that since they're pull-ons, that they would be be too stretchy or thin. They are 68% cotton, so that helps deliver the weight I like. As I am returning them, I will never know if they get majorly loose or saggy in the crotch after wear/ washing. LOVE that all the pockets are functional. Love the dark wash. They are form fitting in the bottom, thighs and waist. No gaping at the back. Waist lays right at the bellybutton. Wish there were belt loops in case you wear a shirt that reveals the top of the pants. I love wearing pull on jeans but I don't want anyone to know they are! Again, I am sad they aren't a true "Long" otherwise I'd give them 5 stars.

Capri-length is tough for petites, especially those of us on the shorter end of that spectrum, and these fall at a somewhat awkward spot on me at 5’ tall. I think I could get away with it if it didn’t bell from the knee down. It doesn’t appear to flare that much in the product photo, so they almost seem like different jeans … I understand the model’s legs are slimmer but the cut should be the same. Bring so wide right below my calf isn’t flattering (looks even wider in real life than in this photo). I usually wear between a size 6 and 8 and ordered these in an 8 and they fit but are pretty snug in my waist/belly. If you’re between sizes or find that you need more room in that area, I would recommend sizing up. There is definitely a lot of stretch, however, and I felt sucked in. The fabric is quite nice. It’s a quality pair of jeans, but if you’re petite, they may not be the jeans for you.

Currently 21 weeks pregnant, 5' 9", 145#. this is my second baby so my belly is a lot bigger this time around and I needed maternity scrubs. I found that the Cherokee maternity scrubs were very well fitted except for the fact that the belly band was way too tight and by the time I was nine months they would be impossible to wear. I have the same problem with these scrubs as other reviewers that the space in between the tummy and your legs is very big, however, once I put them on with a maternity shirt you can't tell the difference anyway. The good thing about them being a little big is I definitely have room to grow and they are super comfortable. Far more comfortable than the Cherokee were. I was going to try them in a small but I ultimately decided that the medium would fit me the best all the way through my pregnancy.

I would recommend these pants because I find them to be comfortable and stylish overall.

Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Even when I saw that these Cropped Corduroy Pants were 100% polyester I was not dissuaded from ordering them. This is because they are from Yesno, a brand that I own pieces from and wear often. If I didn't know that these were polyester pants, I wouldn't know. The fabric feels nice and soft, if pressed I would have guessed that they are a cotton blend. As is usual with this brand, these pants are very well made. They have roomy slash pockets in front and an interesting dropped yolk detail at the back waistband. The belt loops are nice and large so that a wide belt can be worn with them. The waist is mostly elastic and very stretchy, and the zipper and buttons at the front are nice quality. I'm very happy with the way these pants are constructed, the feel of the fabric and the rustic brown color. Sadly, for all I love about these pants they do not fit properly. They fit me, just not properly. There is a dropped crotch which was not mentioned in the listing. So if I have the pants fastened at my belly button or just above, the crotch is hanging two or three inches below that. This makes the rear end look weird because it puckers horizontally by the back seam. They do look better if I pull them up, but then the waist is right under my bra line. Even though a dropped crotch is not my preference, I would still wear them except the fit is not comfortable either. The back seam just rests against my hiney and somehow feels tight... Which is very strange since I'm pretty flat back there. So, even though the fit is not working for me these pants will probably work for others and I think are not a bad deal at $34.99. If you have a longer torso and more booty, these might be your pants! For reference, I am 5'8" 190 lb, and ordered the XL.

Every girl needs a good pair of sweatpants and I had a pair that i’ve loved for 5+years— they’ve become very tattered and old so it was time for a new pair. I am 5’2 and roughly 145lbs curvy athletic build, and they have a little bit extra room but are cuffed at the ends so not super long. I think if you are a tall girl or are longer in the legs, these might be too short. Only negative thing i have to say is they are sooo high waisted on me. Fantastic fit. I’m 6’ tall 201 lbs w/30” inseam 34” waist 44” hips 28” thighs!!👀👀👀 I am ALWAYS in two sizes up to fit my thighs and butt. This leaves me so much gap and nothing to hold up the pants on my giant thighs. Add large calves, and you basically just walk around holding your pants up or wear a belt and try to tuck and conceal all the extra waistband. These jeans are for curvy girls with a waist—OR NOT. My friend tried them on. She carries her weight through the middle and is maybe one size up from me, was able to put them on and zip them up. I was wearing a 16 and ordered a 16. They went on like butter and fit every curve of me. Very comfortable and easy to bend, sit and move. They loosen up quick, so grab a belt after first day wear. They’ll need a hot-water tightening often. I went ahead and ordered a 14 in the light shade. Both fit so well, I’m debating the12 now. 🤔 Stylish? So many compliments already. As for stamina—how well will they hold up over time? 🤷‍♀️ I’ll try to update in a few months.

Fantastic, I'm very happy with these. Got the large and fits well, For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 195 pounds. I measured the unstretched inseam at 28", this is 4 inches longer than LinvMe. I easily put these on and pull up to my crotch. The are skin tight and see-thru. Not near as tight as other brands. The large is like XL in other brands. Thighs fit good on my 22 thigh. leg holes at hem were large enough to get over my size 11 men feet. Hem is 1/2-inch. I usually end up letting the hem out to gain that extra length and then add on another leg finish with either elastic band or stretch lace. easy to find 2 and 3 inch wide elastic bands. Stretch lace easy to find 6 inch wide. Waist is mid waist height, comes to just under my belly button. Rolls down to natural waistline. I roll over the waistband twice for the perfect front rise. Glad I took a chance on these, they are superb value.11-14-24 Bought again, still like, added photos. Even though the length seems short via measurement (I normally want at least a 30 inch inseam for my height) the length stretches during wear and is longer than the flat measurements. This pair I measured the inseam at 27" - fabric stretches so easily I think I stretched it a little to get the 28" measured before.

Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

Finally, I found a pair of denim shorts that I can comfortable wear year round! Generally, I avoid denims because they are difficult to fit properly and are too hot to wear in the warm climates I always live in. But, I always thought denims look good and go with just about every top. This pair of Awuka denim shorts solved my problem. The material is very stretchy and doesn't bind anywhere. The cut is modest being high waisted and with the hem length. The pockets are deep enough. The stitching is good. I did trim off the fray strings at the hem as that's not me. Importantly, the material is not hot. I machine washed and tumbled dry and it didn't shrink. The small fits me quite well. My waist is 28 inches, weight is 120 lbs and height is 5'5". I would purchase this again.

Finally, some knee length shorts! That is what I really like about them. So much so that I also got them in black and navy. These shorts remind me of the ones we wore back in the day, and I was happy to have found them. There are a lot of different shorts on this listing, so it can be confusing. This review is for the white shorts with the buffalo check print that you can fold up. From my experience, this style is the longest they carry. I found them to run a little loose, and there is elastic in the back. They sent a little belt with it, but I haven’t even taken it out if its package yet. I recommend following their size chart. These white shorts are not see through with nude underwear! I am including a photo of me wearing them today and I tucked the back of my HOT PINK shirt into them so you can see for yourselves. The one thing I personally dislike about these shorts is the brown buffalo check material that is sewn on. Thankfully I can wear it unfolded and not have that show. Unfortunately, it kinda does show through just a tiny bit, although my husband said he didn’t notice. Also it buttons where you fold it up, and the buttonhole is not done that well. I am letting these things slide since I am happy to have a decent pair of white shorts that are long enough. For reference, I am 5’9”, 134 lbs, and I bought size small.

Finally...a pair of jeans that fit perfectly and feel amazing! The Silver Jeans Co. Women's Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Trouser Leg Jeans are a high-end high-quality pant. They are well made with "real" denim that is heavy weight and durable. The double button and zipper fly is attractive. The fit is amazing. I don't have many curves at 5'6" and 120 lbs. Most women's size pants fit terribly so I am doomed to wearing leggings 90% of the time or juniors clothing. These jeans are the answer to my pant needs! There is a very slight stretch to the denim...ever so slight...that is a really nice plus. I will likely buy an additional pair in another color. These are fabulous! Bravo!! I am so happy to have found a pair of jeans that fit great, look fantastic, are high quality, durable, and well made.

Finding shorts that don't ride up, don't have a huge gap in the back, and have the right amount of stretch to hold their shape while staying comfortable is practically unheard of for me. I'm 4'11",120lbs, with a 26inch waist and 36inch hips, so I have some bodacious curves which like to make anything and everything ride up on me or fit in unflattering ways. I ordered a small and these shorts fit perfectly!!! The only things some might not like is the way the buttons are pulled, the "frumpy fit" I tried to capture in the second picture, or if 26inches around the waist is pushing it for you definitely size, because if these shorts were even an inch smaller around the waist they wouldn't fit on me.

Finding the right fit for jeggings can be a tall order when you are 5'9" with long legs, but these white jeggings in size 6 Long were a fabulous find! I took them on a trip to Italy in March, and they were a dream come true. Not only did they fit my long legs perfectly, but they were also incredibly comfortable and flattering, making them the ideal travel companion. Worn on the stunning Amalfi Coast, they matched the beauty of the surroundings and handled everything from long walks to leisurely dinners. The jeggings maintained their shape and comfort throughout the trip, proving to be both durable and stylish. For anyone looking for great-fitting, chic, and comfortable jeggings that cater to taller frames, I can’t recommend these enough!

First and foremost, these are true to size with a little extra stretch for those who adjust weights now and then. These have a looser fit on me, but not in a saggy way, but not hip huggers. No issues with bunching or pinching, making it uncomfortable, or any friction when trying to rush around. I have full-sized pockets, I can fit my huge cell phone in any pocket I want, and sit down!! The fabric holds its shape without slipping down throughout the day, I wear these even with the looser fit without a belt. Even on the second-day wear!! The legs are nice and long, so I can cuff the bottoms to have a stylish cuff on the bottom, or let them stay long with strong bottom hems. The length is perfect for me too, barefoot they hit the ground with ¼ inch to spare. That is the perfect length for me, I am 5’7, about 165. The fabric is nice and thick in denim, the lining is secure the whole way down the legs without any separation. The stitch-work is solid without any loose threads and the button holds tight so I don't have concerns about it slipping out like some other stretch jeans.

First and foremost, what I love most about these corduroy pants is the inseam. Too often, pants are manufactured with a 28 - 30 inch inseam, which would be TOO SHORT for the average woman (with a woman's curves). I ordered a size Small; I'm 5'5", 140 lbs and any pants I'd wear would need to be AT LEAST 32" to avoid being floods, particularly if wearing heels. These are the perfect length and I couldn't be more pleased. Great job to the manufacturers. 5 stars.

First dress was too small in bust. Large was ordered which is my usual size. I decided to try a 3-x which was way too big all over. Exchanging for 2-x. Update: This is an xl and I still had a lot of room in it. It’s been washed but not dried and steamed because it was hecka wrinkled. I suggest cuffing sleeves because they are super long. I am 5’9” and 190lbs.

First I love the look and feel of these skinny jeans. I am normally a 18 and ordered the XXL, however the crotch is stretched too tight and so there is a gap there. I am 5’4” and 217#. I am long waisted with a lot of my weight in the belly area. I am going to try sizing up to the 3X but I often have this problem with being between sizes. I will update my review after ordering the larger size. **Update** I have worn the larger 3x size now twice and I can’t rave enough about these jeans. They are really comfortable and you don’t have to constantly pull up the waist. They sit above the belly button on me. I will be buying more of these and I highly recommend them but I would size up if you are tummy and bottom heavy.

First I will say the color does not look like the picture. (I ordered the Jenna) but not a deal breaker for me. love how they fit and that they are long. Personally I would love about 1/2 inch to an inch longer but these will be just fine. For reference I’m 5’10 and wear a 18.
First I will say… very comfortable. They do run large. I 5’8” and these jeans were very long and baggy. I am keeping for comfort? But would not purchase again. Looks a bit sloppy.

First of all these are just jeggings. They were $26.00 and the seller charged $23.00 to shipped them. Although I have Amazon prime some how a shipping fee was charged. Not cool! On top of it all they are 3 sizes too small. My 13yr old daughter couldn't even fit them.

First of all these are super cute. I am 5’2 so I have to buy short length pants otherwise they are just so long. I got these today and tried them on and they are SOOOO LONG! I double checked the pants tags to be sure I actually received the 12s and not regular or tall. They say they are the short version and it’s super frustrating because they’re so cute and no way would these be considered short

First of all, I am 5’10” and very athletic. I weigh 138 lbs. I knew that these would probably be too short. But I thought maybe in a cute way they could work! I am a perfect size 6. I ordered an 8 based on my previous experience with garments made in China. The thighs were uncomfortably tight and the waist was an inch too big. The rise was SUPER HIGH. I’m tall and they were way above my belly button. Plus they pooched out in the tummy area making me look plump there, which I’m not! Yes, they were too short. I could have worked with that, but overall I just couldn’t see anything flattering about these for my body type. My daughter took them and says they are okay for her. She’s super super skinny and my height. They fit baggy and are likes that they are short. Anyhow, there you have it. Skinny girls will look great in these.

My only complaint is that the website displayed pictures of socks that were grayish tan, and the ones I got were a definite beige color. That's okay, I don't mind. They are long, and they bunch a little bit at my knee. When I tried to fold them over as they are displayed in the picture they do not tend to fold neatly, so I just kind of ease the socks over the length of my legs. That's about it, I hope this is .

first of all, ignore my terrible pics…but those give you an idea of my body shape. i usually stick to one brand of jeans bc i’m 37, hate my body, and don’t understand how to dress myself lol. anyway, i keep seeing these ads so i just figured i’d at least try them. i will say, they’re very comfortable. i’m 5’3, about 138lbs but have big hips/butt. i genuinely don’t like wearing tight clothing and need to feel fabrics before buying but these are good. they’re high waisted, which i must have missed in the description bc im dumb, but that’s fine. since i am on the shorter side, they are a bit long, but nothing i can’t manage by rolling the bottom. since they are longer, they seem a bit bigger in the calf area (which you can see in pics). it’s nothing that would make me want to return since i know these will shrink and fit me just fine.

First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

First of all, these are not the color I was expecting!! I ordered grey, but I felt like I got 80’s hard rock jeans or something!!! I will be returning. I will say that I liked that it had belt loops and a button, which most jeggings don’t. I ordered a small and they fit ok. Might have been a little on the long side even though I’m 5’5.

First off I want to say these are not short friendly. These are definitely for my 5 foot three and up girls because they are very long as you can see in the picture but I do love the adjustable straps if you learn how to button them just right as you can see, you can create more of a cuff look which fits a little better but these are perfect for a taller girl or even a petite woman and a 3 inch to 5 inch heel. The waistband is absolutely perfect. It fits perfectly on my smaller size waist while keeping the jeans very baggy and form. Perfect for any streetwear or grungy outfit.

First off I will these fit me perfectly. I got size medium and I’m 5’4”, 130 lbs. they are meant to be high waisted, it sits comfortably right above my belly button and the length is perfect. If you’re average height or taller these probably won’t fit right. I’m very impressed with the quality and weight of the fabric. Sage green is true to the pictures and no issues with them being see through. Definitely ordering another pair

First off I would say that are the most comfortable stretch dress pants I have come across. They have cute little pockets in the front and elastic waistband. This product is true to size. I am 5'3. I weigh 165 lb and I have big hips and got a large but I could have got a medium.

First off I’m on the shorter side (5 foot 3) and weigh 120 pounds. I love the flare look but usually I have to have them hemmed which is annoying, these are perfect! They barely touch the ground with my tennis shoes on! So cute and the material is so soft and there is slight tummy control as well. The back pockets are so adorable and the scrunch on the butt is perfect (some pairs are too much and ride up) these don’t ride! I ordered another pair! So cute and really fun colors as well.

First off the green color is completely different than in photos, it is more of a brown green not army green. I am 5'9" 125lbs, I ordered a medium and it is tight in the shoulders and too short in the arms. The cut on the top shoulder area is very strange. It is not oversized at all as advertised. Material is okay, alot of loose strings where stiching is. Mainly around the bottom of the shaket

First off the sizing on this was great as I am a 29” waist and got the medium and as you can see I have this up on my natural waist and I’m on one of the middle holes so hopefully I’ll be able to use it lower on my hips without issues too. I love the buckle because it’s truly an accessory that elevates your outfit rather than just some thing functional. The faux leather is smooth and clean and doesn’t take away from the buckle’s design.

First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

First off, my bad for not reading the full description that everything is printed on these leggings -- they're not jeans in any way except the look of the printing. I tried them on anyway, and my bf said the dark part in the front looked like I'd peed my pants. My friend said it looked like I was wearing a cod piece. They are soft, I'll give them that. But I was looking for actual jeans, not jeans-look that was printing on shiny leggings. As far as size goes: I'm 5'8, 145 without much in the curve department. I usually wear a medium, but went with L/XL based on others' reviews, and this seemed to be the right size.

First off, the description said machine washable, the label says simply one word “ Hand”. Doesn’t say what the material is on the label. But it’s very rough, the last pair of pants I had like that, snagged on everything. Also the zipper is very hard to zip, I think it’s the wrong size because it bulges, they also seem bigger than a small, I’m going to wash them in the machine to see if they will fit better. The price was good the pictures looked good, but………..I’m 5/4 and weigh 108 if you want to compare. I guess they’ll be ok to hang around the house, but I doubt I’ll be wearing these in public unless they wash up good.

First off, the fabric is different from what I expected. It's a denim, canvas-type material. However, they are so comfortable and cute! These pants exceeded my expectations. They are perfectly baggy and look great with a body suit or cropped tank. These cargos are great pair of pants at a reasonable price for a trendy style. I'm 5'3, 115#, and wearing an XS.

First off, the pants fit wonderfully! I'm 5, 2, weighing 110 lbs, and I got medium-sized pants. It's a bit tight, however the waist band of the pants is quite stretchy. So it fits perfectly with me. First off, these are a solid black jean (not the worn look, as shown in the advertising pics). I love the rich black color and the fact that the fleece lining is also solid black. The lining is so soft, it is like wearing your favorite blanket. These are pretty true to size, although the waistband is a little tighter than I like and they are pretty long (I'm only 5'2"). That said, they are very comfy and fit throughout my curves nicely. These will be great for winter.

First off, these took about a month to be delivered. Ok, so here’s what I think. If you’re someone with hips and a tush, like me, it’s hard for harem pants to work. I went with my regular size medium (I’m 5’2” approx. 140lbs) going by the size chart of a 45” hip I thought these would be plenty large to give a looser more oversized fit. Not the case. The fabric is too thick, and I honestly should have sized up a size or even two, to get the look in the product photos. Honestly, even then, I just don’t know if these would be as stylish as I was hoping they’d be. They are pretty comfortable. The fabric is thick and doesn’t breathe though, so I’d say these are for cooler temps. I am sure I’ll find a way to style these and make them cute, but I was hoping to not have to put too much work into what should be an easy pant to style. All in all, these are a pass for me.

First off, use the size chart. I NEVER take a size 12-14 (waist around 29.5") and I'm usually about an 8. 5'4" and 145 lb. I trusted the size chart and ordered a Large (12-14), and they are a good fit at the waist and tight through the thighs. They seem to be the right size, although I find it really annoying how the jeans keep slipping down and I end up needing to hike them back up again. I think maybe it's because they're so tight at the thighs; it may be pulling them down at the waist. I'm not super crazy about these, and because of the falling-down problem, I probably need to wear a belt (ugh) and I won't be reaching for these first. They're so-so for me.

First problem: the description says "mid-rise", yet the picture is of jeans that are CLEARLY LOW RISE. Honestly I was looking for low rise, but decided I would settle for mid-rise if that's what I received. Ordered a size 7 first (5'5", 135 lbs) which was WAY too small, plus the tags on what they sent me all said "High-rise". Returned those and ordered size 9 thinking the high-rise part was just a fluke. Nope. They sent me high-rise again (as can be seen in the picture) and on top of that, they are slightly loose in the waist, which means they would be falling down in no time. Sending these back for a refund. Won't be ordering again.

First red flag. The pants arrived in the clear warehouse plastic bag. No Amazon packaging or added protection. Tried them on- they’re at least a full size too big. I’m a size 6, 7, or 28. 150lbs hourglass shape. Tiny waist big hips. The waist actually fit alright. The pockets, zipper area and thighs were baggy. Thought maybe I could wash them to tighten up. But then I saw a HUGE HOLE on the inner thigh… if I do replace I would get a size 5. And make sure it comes in Amazon packaging. Genuinely disappointed

First thing I noticed was the huge hole torn in the crotch of the jeans. They are also HUGE in the thigh area, especially when compared to the waist size. Lastly if you are any shorter than about 5' 10"- 6" ft tall they will be WAY too long.

First thing I noticed when I put these on is that the elastic part is really tight (I'm 8mo pregnant, 5'6", 148lbs, and ordered the sm), wondering if that'll still be an issue after the first wash. Otherwise, flattering and comfy, would recommend.

First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

First things first the fit is NOT a perfect shape! I got the size 2 The gap in the back is going to drive me nuts!! Overall not impressed. Edit: I would like to add that as im at work I just lost an entire seem in the rear end. DONT buy these they are NOT worth your money.

All in all I give it a YES 🙌🏼, worth cost and trendy/comfortable enough to make a super cute outfit.

First things first, I normally wear 12-14 jean size and I'm 5′ 2″ tall and weigh 190lbs but I have grown in belly size so hope this helps others choose your size. (I bought the Light Wash3 in size 1X) I am now 5 months pregnant so my normal day-to-day jeans won't budge. These jeans are the first jeans that have fit me without constricting my body, belly or legs got some great stretch to them and also don't drag past my feet, they're perfect length. Not to mention they're not baggie on me making them a lot more flattering than most maternity pants I've worn. I am extremely shocked with how perfect these jeans fitted me and how amazing i felt in them, for $20 dollars these are the best jeans I've owned in my opinion, even though some say they're cheap looking or feel cheap, not to me NOT FOR 20 dollars! High end maternity jeans are way over 50-100 dollars nowadays so these are great for my budget. The belly band is long enough it reaches right below my bra and is very stretchy but not loose, I still have 4 months to go so i hope they'll fit then. I will make sure to buy different colors and update this review accordingly. Now for the negatives (not a huge deal breaker but important to note) These jeans have no front pockets, they're fake pockets which was a disappointment but for some dumb reason women pants normally don't have pockets so im used to it (I wish they would add pockets though, please do!) Another negative is the color is a bit different from the model, mine were a bit lighter in color but honestly not a huge deal for me. Third negative for me is that I expected them to have a hem at the end like in the picture of the model but also something that isn't a huge deal. Lastly, they did have a strong chemical smell like some reviews have mentioned, but the smell reminds me of mothballs so I assume they use some kind of moth control to keep their jeans protected, after I washed them the scent faded away. All in all, these negatives are not a deal breaker whatsoever, so I will definitely get more!

First time ever that I have ordered a pair of jeans online. I have always been one to go and try them on in the store before buying, but with the stretch waistband and spandex in them, I decided to take a chance. I am 5'4" and 180-ish so I ordered the X-Large. I have a problem with so many in the pants line being too long due to my height. Figured with these it might work out to be just about right since they are supposed to be cropped. I was right! They are nearly a normal length on me, and I do not mind that it alters what the style was meant to be at all, lol. I dress for comfort, not fashion. These jeans are very soft and have a forgiving stretch that makes them super comfy to wear. I was surprised at how thick and heavy in weight they are. They will be nice and toasty warm when cooler weather gets here and in the winter. First pair of jeans too that I have ever been able to tuck in a shirt and did not have a spare tire hangover at the top of the jeans. These jeans are well made and look like they will last a long time. I can highly recommend these jeans. If I see a good sale on them, I will be grabbing a second pair, I like them that much! Very happy I took the chance on them.

First time I even got an Amazon brand jean (normally I’m an express girl). I’m 5’7” and 120 so I purchased size 0 regular. The long would have been a mistake! Make sure to measure your proper waist bc these are medium/high rise. I’m gonna wear these with ankle booties, heels, my Tom’s or cowgirl boot. Thinking of getting more colors.

First time wearing boot cut jeans in years and these did not disappoint!! I would like to say that you should size down 1 or 2 sizes, I’m 5’4 and weigh 123lbs and went for the small, I had to use a belt as their was a lot of waist room left, I believe the xs would’ve probably been better for me. Other than that I loooooovvve these jeans, they are so cute, I wore them for Valentine’s Day and got tons of compliments on them!!

First, and most importantly, they fit me really well around the waist. These are the first pants I've owned since having a baby that I can say that about, but these are able to somehow hold onto my belly and not fall down or dig in. I think maybe it's the stretchy material and wide the ruched waistband. I'm 5'10" so finding pants long enough for me is a challenge. These are good because they are cropped and I don't think there's a particular length they have to reach like regular pants. That said, these are longer than I expected. They have not made me feel like I'm too tall to wear them. Second, and almost as important as fitting well, they are comfortable. Like mega comfortable, because they are super soft and lightweight and stretchy. If you are looking for structured pants, look elsewhere. If you want pants that you can get down in the floor to play with your kids, wear all day painlessly, and not bother to change into pajamas at bedtime because they are just that comfortable, these are for you. Third, how they look. I personally don't know what to do with these style wise. I don't own any short or cropped shirts (I'm tall haha). I think they would look cute if the waistband was more visible, but I can't style it that way without updating my wardrobe. So for the looks of the pants, I've knocked off a star simply because I don't know what to wear with them that would be a cute outfit. Fourth, the functionality of these pants surprised me. They went through the wash well with the settings I use for all my clothes (cold/cold wash, extra low dry for 1 hour, spin without heat for a while to cool). The fit didn't seem to change after washing (I have only washed once so far). The pockets are generous in size, my (fairly large) phone fits all the way inside with plenty of extra room at the top to keep it from falling out when I sit. I can even do things like sit on the floor and not only does the phone stay securely in my pocket the whole time, even when getting up, it is comfortable and I don't feel the phone jabbing my hip or anything when I move or sit. Also, the pants stay out on my waist even when adding the weight of the phone to the pocket. Over time, the waistband may get slightly crooked from the uneven load, but it doesn't make the pants fall down or hang cattywompus. I haven't found any lounge pants or even jeans that perform so well at comfortably and securely holding my phone in a pocket. Usually they do well at one (if that!) but not both. I am a SAHM and these pants work really well for my lifestyle. They are very casual, so I don't know if I feel comfortable styling them for wearing on outings. I've primarily used them at home. If you like this wide leg flowy style, for sure give them a try.

First, disappointing that this pair was still long even though I ordered a “Short.” For reference I am 5’2. Second and most importantly, returning this because as I was trying it on I suddenly got stabbed by something sharp— come to find out it was a broken needle that was left in the seam of the pants.

First, I bought the denim pair then loved them so much that I bought the white pair! I did size up since I like mine to fit loose. Great when washed and air dried - didn’t shrink! I’m 5’6”, 155lbs, with big hips and ordered an XL.

First, I cant believe I am sharing such candid pics but I feel us thicker ladies never can tell what the right size is to buy so I hope this helps y'all. (This is my dads acct btw) Ok, to I am 5'6" and currently weight 182 lbs, all of my weight is in my tummy and thighs, I am only a 38B. I go between a 12 and 14 in jeans. I'm pretty sure I remembered some of the reviews saying to size up. I originally ordered size 16 bc I didnt want them skin tight, but they were WAY to big. Sent those back and tried a 12. PERFECT. I can still easily get in and out of the front pockets and I don't feel squished by the waistband when I sit down. I am absolutely getting more of these in different colors so long as they don't shrink in the washer and dryer. If that happens I will for sure update. Good luck ladies!

The pants have a baggy barrel cut which is generally not flattering on me. I am 5’6, 140 pounds and size 6 fits perfectly. I got these with a 20% off coupon and a flash sale, so the value for the price is fantastic.

First, let me state that I have a tiny mirror so my pictures aren’t great. I am also 65, and you can judge for yourself whether I’m too old for this trend. My philosophy is you are almost never too old, but you do you. I purchased the Frida in a 26, which is my normal size and they fit perfectly. I’m only 5’3” and 100 lbs, but have longer legs, so while these are a bit long I like the length. They don’t drag on the ground with casual shoes but can still be worn with heels or boots. I like the front seam detail and the pockets are roomy for women’s denim. I’m loving this baggier style, although I think since I’m small I’ll wear something tighter on top rather than a blousy top and baggy bottom. Live and learn no matter your age. They are super comfortable. I’m usually ready to put sweats on when I get home-COVID ruined me for real clothes, but I can wear these relaxing. I am, in fact, resting on the couch wearing these as I type. Another good buy from Amazon the Drop. I would say this is my new favorite place to get denim.

First, the positive! They fit as I expected. I'd read that many other purchasers sized down. I did not. I am 5 ft 7 in, 148 lbs. I got a size 8. The wait band is pretty tight. First…I’m a leggings girl, I wear them everyday and have been searching for a pair of jeans (ug) that feel comfortable like my leggings do and I feel good wearing. Firstly, I usually don't create lengthy/detailed product reviews on Amazon --unless I feel as if my review may help someone. For starters, I'll describe myself for a more "personalized" review. I'm around 5'5", 140 lbs, with a small waist and thicker bottom half. My wardrobe consists mainly of mid to high waisted jeans/shorts. Needless to say, rarely do I find jeans/shorts that fit me perfectly in both the waist and bottom half. Most of the time, they'll fit well in one area OR the other. Additionally, I dislike everything to do with typical belts on me or with any outfit I wear. I dislike the look, the uncomfortable feeling of it, and especially the buckles. After purchasing some new jeans recently, I ran into my normal "awkward fit" problems. I thoroughly researched many clothing sites that I liked, and Amazon, for regular belts that I may like a little. After having no luck, I thought to myself, "People have created products to help those needing their pants to be larger in the waist, so surely, some genius must have invented a magical product, other than the normal belt, to solve the opposite problem." Then, I found this gem and decided to give it a try. I received it today, so this is my first time wearing it. I'll say, it is pretty magical. Not only does it hold my pants at my waist, but it's also discrete. My only complaints may be that it's not very fashionable and it tends to ride up a tad, but not a huge problem given that it's doing its job well and because I can hide it. I'll end with this statement: if you're like me and suffer from the same "awkward pants fitting" problems, try this out.

Fit. I am 5’9” and I like to have a 33-34 inch inseam on my pants so that, you know, they are pants! I weigh 150 pounds. I normally like a 29” waist. I bought the medium based on people writing that they are 5’9” and 5’10” and such, and that they fit great. Okay, yes, the waist is comfortable and will fit almost any woman’s body because it is elastic and the fabric is stretchy (I bought the black pair). The legs though, do not fit tall women. The inseam is all the way stretched out here in the photo and it is not even 27 inches. (Inseam is the measurement from the bottom of the crotch to the end of the pant leg). My tall friends, do not order these pants! A lot of ankle and leg will be showing! The measurement from the top of the waist to the crotch is 15 inches, and I would consider them somewhat high waisted. Plenty of room for some “junk in the trunk.” The elastic around the bottom of the leg is not too tight, but may be if you have larger calves. Fabric. Overall, the fabric is soft, not see through, and I can see them fitting great for someone who does not have my freakish long legs. Very comfortable and I debated keeping them for pajamas. Care and washing. The tag says they are hand wash only, lay flat to dry - and that they are 95% rayon and 5% spandex. Made in China. I would recommend these pants to people with normal leg lengths.

Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6 (usually) or 28 jeans, long torso. Checked size chart (garment measurements), my waist/hip measurement is between the size 6 and 8. Ordered size 6 expecting a little stretch. Fits comfortably snug in waist, hip and upper half of my butt, falls fairly straight down from there. My suggestion- order the size that includes your measurements at, or up to the next size. For example, order size 6 if your waist is from 26.8"-28.3". Order size 8 if your waist is 28.4"-30", order size 10 if your waist is 30.1"-30.7", and so on. These are very high waisted even on my lanky torso; when I sit down the waist hikes up above my belly button. I like that just fine, but something to consider if you are more petite. Legs are shorter than I'd prefer which is expected, because I am too tall for regular sizes and too short for special tall sizes. It's super annoying but I'm sure there would be more complaints if they were longer so can't exactly blame the pants. Materials/Design - Maybe the other colors use different denim, but I wouldn't call this thick, scratchy old-school denim. I'd describe it as midweight, slightly stretchy but holds its shape well all day, and relatively smooth. Not crazy about the whiskers, but not a dealbreaker. Considering how many pairs of jeans I have to try on in a store before finding one I like, was pretty surprised at the quality and fit of these. I take time to write honest reviews that I would find , whether favorable or not.

For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

For context I'm 5'7 190# 52 yo... I am very picky about what I wear for many reasons, first being I am a gen xer in my 50s which means I need to still be stylish and somewhat boho but respectable for my age! Iykyk! So I need my pants to be comfy, lightweight, extra cute but not too young and wearable to the office as much as home. I have tried at least a dozen different pairs of pants from Amazon this year and most of them have gone right back to the return pile. So when the shipment of these rust bread linen pants came I rolled my eyes and through the bag on my bathroom dressing table without a second thought. Fast forward to this morning... At the moment I tried them on a ray of light entered the room and there rose a choir of angels singing.... Yes! These are PERFECT in every way! Color is interesting, stylish yet subtle!! Wide leg not shapeless! Perfect hit at ankle! Pockets but not obvious! Elastic waistband provides support, not bulge!!! I love them enough and I have struggled enough to find the right pair that I decided to actually post a photo of me wearing them which made me cringe but I want you to be happy as I am right now! Omw to order one(or two) in every color....

For context, I’m a 41 year old female, 5’4”, 175 lbs, recently lost 45 lbs and somewhat athletic build. I’ve had 4 c-sections and have a bit of a belly and the ol’ caboose is a little big (not overly though). Originally, I ordered the 13/14 (waist) x 30 (inseam) in the Dark Indigo. I’m short, but the 30 inch inseam was about 2 inches off the ground - higher if I bent or sat down. Too short for my taste. Legs were too tight for my liking throughout the thighs, calf and especially the knees. The waist was too tight as well. The first time I tried them on, the waist was okay-ish, but when I went to try them on again for a double check, I had eaten a small lunch and they were extremely uncomfortable/restrictive. Amazon allowed me to exchange them for the same pair in a different size, so I ordered and just received 15 (33 inch waist) x 32 (inseam) which luckily, was right before I was leaving to return the first pair (cuz these ones are going back with them). Fabric is that thinner, saggy-type stretch where if you wore them for an hour or two they’d start drooping. The first ones I ordered were a sturdier, stiffer jean material which I prefer over super stretchy droopy jean material. The waist line and lower abdomen or ‘Fupa’ area is too poochy. Length is better with the 32 inseam. A little longer than I’d prefer, but something I’d be fine with if the rest of the jeans were decent. One of the biggest issues is the color. The 15 x 32 are waaaay too light to be ‘Dark Indigo’. In the photos I provided, they are the pair on the left. I wouldn’t have kept them even if they fit because I don’t dig light pants on me, hence why ordered Dark Indigo. Definitely inaccurate description (photo on Amazon doesn’t match either). The 13/14 x 30 on the right, I wouldn’t necessarily call Dark Indigo but the wash is really flattering on my frame. Hope this helps.

For context: I'm 5'9", around 130 lbs, and usually a pant size of 26/27. I ordered the Size 4. I was able to fit two fists comfortably between my stomach and the waistline, and they end up riding lower on my hips. Like most work pants I've gotten, they're very long and I'll be hemming them. I will be returning them for a smaller size because I personally don't like how low they fall on my hips and how low the crotch is from that. Otherwise, they fit great! They're snug around the thigh and loose down the leg.

For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

For reference, I am 5’6” 142 lbs with a rectangular torso and thick thighs, I ordered a medium, as suggested, and I don’t have any complaints about the way they fit. They are snug on my body type, because I don’t have much of a waist, but if you are on the skinny side or have an hourglass figure, i’m sure it would be different. There’s nothing overtly wrong with the shorts, but they simply aren’t what I ordered. I included a picture of the color and style for reference, next to what they look like on. I went back and double checked what I ordered, thinking maybe there were two grays and I clicked on the wrong one - but I ordered correctly and didn’t see another one that looks like what I got that it could have been mixed up with. The shorts in the photo look like they’re made of soft, swing-y material, almost like pajamas. The fabric of the shorts I received is stiff and rough. I don’t understand the super heavy-duty waistband paired with an unnecessarily long and superfluous drawstring (it literally does nothing), but I guess it’s not hurting anything. Not worth the hassle of sending them back at this price point, but beware you’re not getting what you think you are.
